en\u001f27.03.2011, Ulrich Plarre: initi: 27 March 2011, Ulrich Plarre: initial feedback, northern meadow
en\u001f27.03.2024, Konrad Mahlknecht: N: 27 March 2024, Konrad Mahlknecht: Still a brilliant landing spot.
en\u001f27.04.2022, Thorsten Grether: In: 27 April 2022, Thorsten Grether: Head towards 12, keeping the distinctive hut with the pointed roof in your sights. The field rises slightly. Come to a halt no later than the diagonally ploughed field, as there is a drop-off there. The field marked in blue on the satellite view is correct. In the image labelled ‘View towards landing direction 12’, I am standing at the end of the field suitable for landing! The image ‘View towards direction 30’ shows the field suitable for landing. I could not see any power lines on approach, so I cannot understand the existing warning.
27 June 2022, Alexander Keupp: 26 June 2022: Good meadow with slight undulations. When approaching from the north, be sure to watch out for the street lights next to the road. These are green and therefore difficult to spot. Approach from the south over the houses (multi-storey).
en\u001f27.11.2022, Michael Henökl: E-Le: 27 November 2022, Michael Henökl: Power line on approach from direction 16 just before the start of the meadow; it is better to approach from direction 34; touchdown point is at the bend in the path. The field slopes slightly towards the south-east and north-east (towards the stream). Not suitable for two-seaters. The approach must be spot on!
en\u001f27.11.2022, Michael Henökl: Fres: 27 November 2022, Michael Henökl: Fresen UL – south-eastern meadow. Former UL meadow. Tree in the upper right-hand corner of the runway. Land only uphill towards the north-east
27 November 2022, Michael Henökl: Fresen municipal works yard – north-western meadow. Agricultural land, slopes upwards towards the east; landings must be made uphill only
en\u001f27.11.2022, Michael Henökl: Höhe: 27 November 2022, Michael Henökl: Altitude 608 m MSL. Direction: East–West. Agricultural land with varied vegetation; one strip is usually left as pasture. Hazards: Buildings on the western edge; power line to the east, approx. 400 m away, running north to south; to the east, a pasture for cattle with an electric fence. Approach from the east or west. Alternative: Passail outlying landing meadow – approach north-south 47.2721 N, 15.5058 E
en\u001f27.11.2022, Michael Henökl: Weid: 27 November 2022, Michael Henökl: Pasture, elevation 628 m above mean sea level, sloping gently towards the south. Approach direction: north–south (usually southerly wind). Hazards: Soft and uneven/bumpy ground; scattered tree stumps to the west of the marked area; a group of trees on the south-western edge. Alternative: Arzberg outfield – approach east–west 47.2645 N, 15.4916 E
en\u001f27.11.2022, Peter Stierlen: Ergä: 27 November 2022, Peter Stierlen: Additional information/details on the Oberkollwangen outfield. Seasonal vegetation. Level in the longitudinal direction. Sloping slightly towards the south in the transverse direction. Landing on 07 is theoretically also possible, but the road in the approach path requires extra caution. In wavy conditions (SW or NE), this is easier than the western or eastern landing fields. No up-to-date information on the condition is available for 2022.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Hans Trautenberg: Be: 27 December 2021, Hans Trautenberg: In the southernmost alternative, the field is shown too far to the east. As it is shown on the map, a fence runs through the field. See also the photos by Mathias Schunk.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Hans Trautenberg: Fa: 27 December 2021, Hans Trautenberg: In some places, the slope drops by 5–8 metres to 250 metres in the 28 direction.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Hans Trautenberg: Im: 27 December 2021, Hans Trautenberg: Still unlandable, and now even partially tarmacked.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Hans Trautenberg: La: 27 December 2021, Hans Trautenberg: The accessible section begins 200 metres west of the row of trees along the village access road.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Hans Trautenberg: Le: 27 December 2021, Hans Trautenberg: Cable run along the northern road, hence the shifted start of the slope.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Martin Dietrich: Auf: 27 December 2021, Martin Dietrich: Given the approach over the houses, and normally with the valley wind at my back, I would give it a grade A at best.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Martin Dietrich: Lan: 27 December 2021, Martin Dietrich: 90 per cent of the approach is with a tailwind (valley wind). Barbed-wire fences have now been erected in the north-eastern half of the landing area marked in blue.
en\u001f27.12.2021, Martin Dietrich: Wei: 27 December 2021, Martin Dietrich: It remains a good option; no further obstacles have arisen.
en\u001f270 Prairies. Detrempe au printe: 270 Meadows. Spring ploughing. Settle on the slope. Option of electric fencing along the plots or the road.
en\u001f270/090 Prairie. Cultures possib: 270/090 Meadow. Suitable for cultivation. Near the point between Merindol and Mallemort.
en\u001f28.02.2005, Christian Holzner: A: 28 February 2005, Christian Holzner: Take off only 15 metres past the path, as there is still a dip here.
en\u001f28.02.2021, Konrad Mahlknecht: A: 28 February 2021, Konrad Mahlknecht: All the information is still up to date.
en\u001f28.02.2021, Michael Herrmann: Bi: 28 February 2021, Michael Herrmann: I drove to the meadow on 28 February 2021; it would have been a good place to land.
en\u001f28.04.2015, Werner Weninger: Bei: 28 April 2015, Werner Weninger: Take care during the approach, as you’ll be flying over a house and some trees. The meadow slopes gently upwards.
en\u001f28.04.2023, Konrad Mahlknecht: D: 28 April 2023, Konrad Mahlknecht: The meadow is still just as described here. From time to time, there may be bales of hay in the meadow.
en\u001f28.05.2021, Albert Kiessling: Di: 28 May 2021, Albert Kiessling: I cannot understand the field length of 700 metres, nor the statement that they are ‘only accessible from the south’. The fields running east-west all cross the direction of the furrows and extend beyond field boundaries. I doubt that these are A-fields. The fields marked in yellow (260 metres long) are fine and are situated right next to the model aeroplane airfield. I dare to doubt that the model airfield itself (46:39:41N 13:01:22E), with a length of 120 metres and a width of 6 metres, is suitable for landing.
en\u001f28.05.2021, Albert Kiessling: La: 28 May 2021, Albert Kiessling: According to Google Earth, the recommended landing direction is downhill. I can’t think of any reason why one shouldn’t land in the opposite direction.
en\u001f28.05.2026, Konrad Mahlknecht: H: 28 May 2026, Konrad Mahlknecht: Visited today; everything remains the same.
28 May 2026, Konrad Mahlknecht: Landed here today. Everything as before. The road is only partially passable; it drops half a metre after that. [ WeGlide flight ]
en\u001f28.07.2023, Alistair Bruce: The: 28.07.2023, Alistair Bruce: The easterly option visited in July 2023 in an LS4. A good alternative to the main marked field. A fence is at the western end and a diagonal trench, both visible from the air. A path splits the field in two segments, this could be safely rolled over if a long landing is made. An approach in both directions possible. Video of landing:
en\u001f28.09.2022, Paul Bernhard: Bis 1: 28 September 2022, Paul Bernhard: Up to 18 metres is probably fine, but anything above that is not an option for me, partly because the approach isn’t exactly straightforward. There is a slight rise in the ground level near the farmyard.
en\u001f28.12.2021, Hans Trautenberg: ne: 28 December 2021, Hans Trautenberg: new photo of the ground.
en\u001f28.12.2021, Tobias Werner: Priva: 28 December 2021, Tobias Werner: Private airstrip, not recorded anywhere else. Assessed from the air in November 2021 as suitable for landing.
en\u001f29.08.2004, Ulrich Plarre: Es fi: 29 August 2004, Ulrich Plarre: Huge earthworks are taking place!!!
en\u001f29.10.2008, Ulrich Plarre: Auf Q: 29 October 2008, Ulrich Plarre: Watch out for overhead power lines during the approach! The field marked on the map so far was only 150 metres long! However, the landing field can be used right up to the western overhead power line, unless there are pasture fences in the way! No ditches!
en\u001f29.10.2008, Ulrich Plarre: Nicht: 29 October 2008, Ulrich Plarre: Not recommended! A last resort! Better to land in Kramsach, Inntal! Caution: When approaching from the south (20°): overhead power lines and a residential building! At the southern end of the meadow, the ground drops steeply for 100 metres! Approach from south to north (02°): clear!
en\u001f29.10.2023, Till Berthold: Weite: 29 October 2023, Till Berthold: Still on top.
en\u001f29.11.2021, Christoph Jütte: Rec: 29 November 2021, Christoph Jütte: On the right, you can see the silver car; at this point, the field narrows by half over the final 70 metres. The edge in the middle of the field is also important. It is best to use the northern (longer) side of the field. The edge is more pronounced at the eastern end and only slightly so at the western end … as you can see in the pictures. Take care when coasting into the narrow part of the field. There is a lighting column at the roadside and a slight embankment towards the road!
en\u001f30.01.2004, Ulrich Plarre: Etwas: 30 January 2004, Ulrich Plarre: A bit tricky! The landing field is divided by two fences! Approach from south to north! The field slopes upwards from south to north! In the northern part of the field, there are undulations right at the northern end! Landing technique: Approach over the central part of the field! Touch down shortly after the fence in the northern field, next to the eastern farm! Livestock farm! If you keep closer to the eastern part of the meadow whilst taxiing – nearer to the road – you’ll avoid a very bumpy patch!
en\u001f30.01.2004, Ulrich Plarre: Lange: 30 January 2004, Ulrich Plarre: Long approach over the waste collection plant on the Hart-Fügen road, with a striking bridge over the River Ziller – running parallel to the high-voltage pylons and the River Ziller! 1 Substation: Touch down after the cross-road near the farmstead lying to the east at right angles! Roll-out between the farmstead and the cross-road at the substation. Approach from the north obstructed by overhead power lines at the model flying site. 2 Model flying site: Touch down after the cross-road at the substation! Taxi out beneath the overhead high-voltage lines in front of the model flying site. The path by the substation cannot be driven over; be aware of the stump in the middle of the approach. The cross-path at the model flying site can be driven over. 3 North: Approach from the north and south; touchdown point east of the farmstead and trees, towards the water ditch. Watch out for the stump. The track curving eastwards can be driven over.
en\u001f30.01.2004, Ulrich Plarre: Wiese: 30 January 2004, Ulrich Plarre: The meadow rises gently from north to south, with a steeper incline at the end! There are solid fences running alongside it! On approach, there are tall trees to the north! Often used by glider pilots from Königsdorf!
en\u001f30.07.2024, Bruno Caprile: The f: 30.07.2024, Bruno Caprile: The field indicated with number 3 was landed on july 31, 2024, by. Fellow pilot Flavio Dal Pan (AVUT, Trento) on Club Libelle. Length is. About 300m, smooth, freshly mowed. The direction of landing was due. North, facing a light breeze. The road is conveniently close. The two. Electric lines indicated in red (dashed line for the lighter one) are. Well visible from the air, run almost parallel to the direction of. Landing, and pose no threat. The flight is on OLC:. In the second image, the position of the glider at landing stop.
en\u001f30.09.2023, Hans Trautenberg: Bi: 30 September 2023, Hans Trautenberg: I’ve flown over the meadow from both directions before, although it was a while ago now.
en\u001f30.09.2023, Rositsa Ivanova: Alt: 30 September 2023, Rositsa Ivanova: An alternative to Hinterwildalpen. The approach from the south is less congested than from the north.
30 October 2021, Dr Arno Hutter: 30 October 21: 2.5 km south-east – a slightly longer alternative route on very flat ground along the cycle path
en\u001f30.11.2021, Juerg Thomas Weiss:: 30 November 2021, Juerg Thomas Weiss: Run-out zone marked by a red and white barrel
en\u001f300 En pente. Atterro face au NW: 300 On a slope. Land facing north-west only. The road is 50 cm higher.
en\u001f300/120 Reco a pied indispensabl: 300/120 Reconnaissance on foot essential = tricky. A south-east-facing landing is recommended.
en\u001f305/125 Ligne electrique partie: 305/125 Power line in the south-eastern part of the fields. Tailwind from the north-east.
en\u001f31.05.2024, Peter Guentzer: Wies: 31 May 2024, Peter Guentzer: Meadow in the condition described
31 May 2024, Peter Guentzer: Meadows in the condition described
en\u001f310 Pente se poser face NW. Tdp: 310 Slope, landing facing NW. Left-hand turn. Base along the mountain. Rotors in a north-westerly wind.
en\u001f310 Poser face NW dans moitie dr: 310 Position facing north-west in the right-hand half of the field. Embankment at the entrance and a ditch at the north-western end. Tailwind on the north-eastern side. Rotors from the north-west.
en\u001f310 Vent arriere cote S du champ: 310 Tailwind on the south side of the field. Meadow.
en\u001f330 Atterro face NW. Bumpy. Ne p: 330 Landing facing NW. Bumpy. Do not use the fields to the south.
en\u001f340 Vent arriere a W du champ. E: 340 Tailwind to the west of the field. On final approach, align with the central row of trees separating the fields to ensure you fly over the power line.
en\u001f340 uniquement. Reco a pied indi: 340 only. You must be able to walk.
en\u001f360/180 Champ Novotel. Attention: 360/180 Novotel field. Beware of power lines. Cereals and maize. Choose according to the crops.
en\u001f360/180 Velisurface treuil. Pist: 360/180 Velisurface winch. Unmarked runway. Tailwind from the east. Preferred landing direction: north.
en\u001fA lot of fence posts, better cho: A lot of fence posts, better choice: Sillian!
en\u001fAL Wiese in Galtür in Ordnung: AL Wiese in Galtür is fine
en\u001fATTN: RWY08 with north/northeast: ATTN: RWY08 with north/northeast wind lee effect due to landfill mountain. Usable in direction 08 from threshold approx. 500 m. Pay attention to uneven ground, especially after rain the field can be very soft! TO/LDG on the south side of the runway, stay on or close to the tarmac strip. LDG 26 with MOT only as a long landing to the hangar. When approaching from the north, please strictly avoid the villages of Hopfen (Kurklinik) and Eschach. Avoid the residential area on departure 08, keep left towards the Festspielhaus. On the cross take-off for motorised aircraft, take off to the middle of Forggensee.
en\u001fAbsolute emergency meadow strong: Absolute emergency meadow strongly rising, not recommended!
en\u001fAchtung, neue Weidezäune auf der: Please note: there are new pasture fences on the west side of the meadow. But otherwise everything’s fine. And there’s still plenty of space.
en\u001fAchtung: bisher keine Bodenbesic: Please note: no site inspection has been carried out to date (as at 3 May 2020); there may be a power line in the southern quarter, so only Category B for the time being! Valley wind (on thermal days with no wider regional wind) from the north.
en\u001fAirport Innsbruck is 19km away a: Airport Innsbruck is 19km away and is 600m deeper.
en\u001fAlles noch so wie Jan beschriebe: Everything’s still just as Jan described. Although I’d say landing over the barn onto the northernmost meadow is a bit of a challenge. You really do need to factor in an extra 20 metres of altitude that you’ll need to lose. I’d go for the second one. Today there was also a yellow windsock standing in the ditch in front of the hut on the eastern side of the road, about 50 metres away. Otherwise, brilliant!
en\u001fAlles wie bisher beschrieben. Di: Everything as described previously. The long meadow is interrupted right at the start by a field, which, however, serves perfectly as a threshold. The others are all bordered by paths, where you must expect damage to the fuselage if the aircraft is overloaded. The middle one would be my choice here, or the one behind it. The paths serve as thresholds.
en\u001fAlternative gleich neben der Aut: An alternative right next to the motorway
en\u001fAlternative nördlich:. Schöne la: Alternative to the north: A lovely, long meadow. The owner isn’t particularly keen on landings, though, as paragliders and hang-gliders are regularly parked there too! When landing, it is therefore advisable to get in touch with the owner via the Stubnerbauer farmyard; the Stubnerhof is right across the road from the meadow and also offers farm holidays. There are other ‘designated’ meadows in the valley.
en\u001fAlternative südwestlich:. Lange: Alternative to the south-west: a long, easily accessible meadow; the northern meadow can also be used, depending on the season, but has a slope on one side in the northern part. The village of Bramberg lies approximately 3 km north-east of this meadow.
en\u001fAlternative südöstlich:. Es gibt: Alternative route to the south-east: There are several landing options here, which should be used before attempting to cross Lake Ossiach to the east towards Feldkirchen by force. The area along the lake is unsuitable for landing!
en\u001fAlternative südöstlich:. Fahrspu: Alternative to the south-east: The runway in the centre of the field can be flown over, but both halves of the field are long enough on their own. Valley wind (on thermal days with no wider-area wind) from the north.
en\u001fAlternative östlich:. Unproblema: Alternative to the east: Unproblematic farmland, although there are a number of power lines throughout the area; consequently, due to a major power line, this field can only be approached from the south!
en\u001fAm 16. April bin ich selbst in F: On 16 April, I made an off-field landing in Farchant-Nord myself. The meadow is flat and wide enough even for a DG1000. Due to the valley wind, a northbound landing is advisable. Contrary to previous comments, I would not overrun any of the cross-runways. The embankments are steep enough to send an aeroplane back into the air even at around 60 km/h. If you get too far, it’s better to deploy the air brakes and fly over the second cross-path. After getting out, I noticed the iron bars used to cordon off the western part of the field. They cannot be seen from the air. If you’re aware of this and aim for the eastern part along the road, it’s no problem. The remaining 35 metres in width are sufficient. A lovely field – I can highly recommend it. It looks even better in real life than the photos suggest.
en\u001fAm westlichen Ausgang des unland: Situated at the western end of the inaccessible Lesachtal valley, this meadow (marked in green) can be approached easily from both sides. It is located south of the hang-gliding and paragliding landing site (marked in yellow), which is equipped with a windsock. The ground is relatively flat and soft, and the meadow is also well suited to heavier aircraft. In the middle of the meadow, to the north and south, there are two small but clearly visible pillars (blue circles), which do not, however, obstruct landing. The obstacles are approximately 40 metres apart, so they pose a problem at most for the Open Class. It is best to touch down in the eastern half. The slopes to the north of this are very thermally active (hence the paragliders) and still offer a good chance of gaining altitude from a low height. Landing at the paragliding site is not normally recommended, as there are too many people standing around there. Purple arrow: best approach to the slope.
en\u001fAn der Westseite steht jetzt ein: There is now a new factory on the west side, which makes the approach from the west considerably more difficult!
en\u001fAnfliegen würde ich in Richtung: I’d approach from the 12 o’clock direction; the terrain rises by 5 metres and you can roll straight under the power line
en\u001fApproach from the south is more: Approach from the south is more clear than from the north.
en\u001fApproach from west sports field: Approach from west sports field with high fence!
en\u001fApproach over 10 m high building: Approach over 10 m high buildings, paths can be rolled over, in the southern part terrain step
en\u001fApproach parallel to the Inn ove: Approach parallel to the Inn over the railroad line, GSM masts
en\u001fAsphaltpiste wird abgetragen, ni: The tarmac runway is being removed and is no longer suitable for landing. There are numerous good fields in the surrounding area.
en\u001fAt the south end it goes 100 m s: At the south end it goes 100 m steeply downhill, Kramsach 8 km and 350m lower
Be aware of paragliding activity. Part of the meadow is used for training.
en\u001fBesichtigung Juni 2021, Zustand: Inspected in June 2021; condition as described. The approach from the west must be steep (sloping terrain, see photo)
en\u001fBesichtigung am 01.06.2024. Leic: Site visit on 1 June 2024. Slightly uphill from east to west. Approach therefore, and due to trees, only from east to west (towards the valley); with a valley headwind, this means a tailwind. Quite a lot has changed compared to Ulrich Plarre’s old photographs: 1. 100 metres past the easternmost edge of the meadow, a track now crosses the meadow at right angles to the landing direction. The track itself would be passable, but during the site visit there was a fence on one side. However, you haven’t actually reached the ground by the time you reach the track. You can treat the new track as a threshold. 2. On the northern edge of the meadow, at the start of this track, there is a newly built barn. Although this makes the meadow appear narrower from the right, it does not otherwise cause any hindrance. 3. The distance from the new track to the substation (called ‘Schober’ by Urs) is exactly 200 metres. However, you can ride past the substation on the left. 4. From the substation to the edge of the woodland, there is a further 100 to 120 metres of overshoot to the edge of the woodland, the westernmost end of the meadow. 5. At the southern edge of the meadow, gravel is either being extracted or used for backfilling (“land filling” according to the signage). In any case, only the northern half of the meadow’s width is usable; the southern half forms a slope descending from south (the southern edge of the woodland) to north, across the direction of flight. Short, narrow, a rather poor B. Inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 01.06.2024. Neue: Site visit on 1 June 2024. New meadow “Lengau West”, approx. 500 m west of the existing “Lenagu” meadow listed in the catalogue. Situated right at the head of the Glemmtal valley; the westernmost flat area in the Glemmtal. A narrow, long meadow between houses to the north and large car parks to the south. Rises gently from east to west. However, due to its length, landing is possible in both directions. When approaching from the east (valley-inwards), a curved final approach is necessary, as four houses have been built into the meadow on the right (north), significantly reducing its width. When approaching from the west (out of the valley), you land on gently sloping ground. 100 metres east of the western end of the meadow, a (natural) stream crosses the meadow from north to south. Like a ditch, this cannot be crossed by vehicle. Nevertheless, there remains a distance of 480 metres between the stream to the west and the crossroad to the east. However, due to the various difficulties, it is clearly only a Category B site, though a perfectly acceptable one. In my opinion, it is actually better than the previous ‘Lengau’ meadow. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 01.06.2024. Nörd: Site visit on 1 June 2024. The northern field is planted with maize again this year; the plants are still short at present and therefore still very easy to land on. The southern meadow remains in good condition. Video of the site visit:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 01.06.2024. Wies: Site visit on 1 June 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition; however, there is a wide choice of equally good, and in some cases even better, meadows in the large Saalfelden valley basin. Video of the site visit:
Inspection on 1 June 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition. Inspection video:

Inspection on 1 June 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition. A long, flat meadow between the road and a power line running parallel to it. Caution: roughly halfway across the meadow, a power line branches off at right angles and crosses the meadow from the triangular (A-shaped) electricity pylon to the bus shelter by the road. This cross-line is difficult to see. Nevertheless, in my opinion, the meadow is Category A, as both landing directions are possible. In both landing directions, one could either land on the first half of the meadow (as seen from the approach direction) and come to a halt before the cross-line, or roll past it, or fly over the cross-line and then land on the second half of the meadow (as seen from the approach direction). Site inspection video:
Inspection on 1 June 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition; clearly Category A. Inspection video:
Inspection on 1 June 2024. Meadow unchanged. Inspection video:

Site visit on 1 June 2024. Meadow unchanged. Slopes gently from east to west. Approach therefore only from east to west (towards the valley); with a valley headwind, this means a tailwind. Final approach over the trees is rather tight. During the site visit, the meadow appeared to be a single, unbroken area; however, on Google Maps, an additional (presumably only temporary) division by pasture fences can be seen in the western part, which would further reduce the size of the already short meadow. Category: more like ‘B minus’. Site visit video:

Site inspection on 1 June 2024. Meadow unchanged. Slopes rise gently from east to west. Approach is therefore only possible from east to west (towards the valley); with a headwind in the valley, this means a tailwind. Category B. Inspection video:
Viewing on 1 June 2024. The field is NO LONGER suitable for landing, as described. Viewing video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 02.11.2024. 1. W: Site visit on 2 November 2024. 1. Western meadow: Largely unchanged as described. The meadow slopes steeply from the north (road) to the south (Buchensteinwand). Landing is therefore only possible from north to south. The crosswind approach must be taken very low and very close to the opposite slope. Extremely short final approach; touch down after the cross-country path; treat the cross-country path as a threshold. Although it may be possible to overshoot the cross-country path as described, this is certainly not recommended and, given the short final approach, is not at all necessary. At the western end of the path stands a new stable building (built in 2022), but this does not cause any hindrance. The surface quality of the meadow is very good and level. The southern (upper) end of the meadow then becomes increasingly undulating; however, thanks to the good gradient, the roll-out distance remains very short and the southern (upper) end of the meadow is not needed anyway. Inspection video: western meadow. 2. Eastern meadow. Unchanged as described. The meadow slopes steeply from north (road) to south (Buchensteinwand). Landing is therefore only possible from north to south. In the middle of the very wide meadow stands a single barn; this is not a problem. Landing is possible both to the left (east) and to the right (west) of the barn, with ample width available. The crosswind approach must be taken very low and very close to the opposite slope. Very short final approach, slightly longer than for the western meadow. It is NOT possible to overshoot the cross-country path as described, but this is not necessary anyway due to the short final approach. The surface of the meadow is very good and level. The southern (upper) end of the meadow forks, as previously described, into two overshoot strips, separated by a small rectangular copse. Both overshoot strips are undulating; the western one is also very steep (like a ski slope). However, thanks to the good gradient, the roll-out distance remains very short and there is no need to use the southern (upper) end of the field anyway. Inspection video of the eastern field:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 02.11.2024. Unve: Site visit on 2 November 2024. Unchanged as described. The meadow slopes gently from west to east. Landing is therefore only possible from west to east. The meadow is large and flat. Landing is also possible north of the marked strip. The crosswind approach must be tight and low along the slope. Therefore only a B, but a very good B! Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 02.11.2024. Wies: Site visit on 2 November 2024. Meadow sloping upwards from the east (village) to the west (edge of the woodland on the hillside). Landing is therefore only possible from east to west. Unobstructed approach; a very long final approach across the entire valley is possible. Approach runs between a residential area of detached houses to the north and a farm to the south. The steady climb presents no difficulties on landing, but keeps the roll-out distance short. Slight cross-slope from north (higher) to south (lower); not a problem. Slight undulations in the ground; not a problem. A few clearly visible marker posts along a field boundary in a direct westerly extension of the farm; not a problem. Quality A due to the unobstructed approach and the helpful climb. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 03.11.2024. Unve: Inspection on 3 November 2024. Still in very good condition, as described by Benjamin Bachmaier on 13 July 2022. Inspection video:
Inspection on 3 November 2024. Unchanged as described. Definitely Category C only, for emergencies only. Inspection video:

Inspection on 3 November 2024. Unchanged as described. The only drawback of this meadow is that the area, which is in itself long and flat, is divided into several plots. There are individual boundary posts along the field boundaries. Without this division, the meadow would be a clear ‘A’; because of the field boundaries, it is only a (very good) ‘B’. Viewing video:

Site visit on 3 November 2024. Unchanged as described. A cul-de-sac with three street lamps divides the meadow into a western and an eastern half. The western half of the meadow slopes upwards from west to east towards the cul-de-sac. Approach to the western half is therefore only possible from west to east. The western half of the meadow is divided transversely by several field boundaries; there are individual marker posts here. The eastern half of the meadow has no longitudinal slope. However, access to the eastern half is still only possible from west to east, as a power line crosses at the eastern end. Access to the eastern half would have to be made flying low over the cul-de-sac and between the street lamps; the distance between the street lamps is more than ample for this. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 03.11.2024. Zahl: Viewing on 3 November 2024. There are numerous good landing sites in the wide, flat Inn Valley, though many of them are situated across the valley. A distinctive feature of the Kramsach meadow is that it lies along the valley axis. It is flat, with no gradient, and offers a completely unobstructed approach from both directions. Site visit video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 04. Juli 2022. U: Viewing on 4 July 2022. Still in good condition, as described. Viewing video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 08.11.2024. Nörd: Site visit on 8 November 2024. Northernmost meadows: These are the neighbouring meadows, listed in the catalogue to date, situated to the north and south of a tarmac farm track. Approach and landing are only possible from west to east. Slightly ascending in the direction of approach from west to east. Both strips are short and divided into small sections. Individual marker posts along the field boundaries. The strip north of the track is likely unsuitable for landing due to these posts and a barn in the approach path. Although the strip south of the track has a clear approach, it is also only Category C due to various posts – for emergencies only. Inspection video of the northern meadows: Middle meadow: New meadow. Approach and landing only possible from west to east. Approach between two barns. Treat the path between the barns as a threshold. Slight and steady incline in the landing direction from west to east. No cross-slope. Flat surface. Very short, therefore only Category B. Inspection video of the middle meadow: Southern meadow: Meadow opened by Jan Zummach on 29 May 2023 (see also his description below). Approach and landing only possible from west to east. Treat the transverse ditch as a threshold. The surface rises in the direction of landing from west to east. No cross-slope. Distinct bump in the ground. Very short, hence only Category B. The best of the three meadows in Ehrwald. Inspection video of the southern meadow:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 08.11.2024. Wies: Site visit on 8 November 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition. The north-eastern of the two marked strips is divided into quite small sections, with individual marker posts at the field boundaries. The south-western of the two marked strips is less subdivided and is therefore better. Depending on where the grass has recently been mown, there may well be further good options to be found in the large and very flat valley floor. Site visit video:
Inspection on 8 November 2024. Meadow unchanged as described. Inspection video:
Site visit on 8 November 2024. Meadow unchanged as described. Area divided into several parcels; individual boundary posts at the field boundaries. Video of the site visit:

Site visit on 8 November 2024. Meadow unchanged. Approach and landing only from east to west. A total of 5 parallel landing strips, all over 300 metres long, level and without any gradient. All strips are separated from one another by clearly visible longitudinal ditches. The northernmost strip is the widest, measuring 22 metres at its narrowest point. Approach over a single barn. The second-northernmost strip is slightly narrower, 20 metres wide, but offers a completely unobstructed approach from the east. The three southernmost strips are significantly narrower. Nevertheless, they are still suitable for landing even with larger wingspans, as no posts are expected along the longitudinal ditches. All strips end in the west at a small hill that rises sharply. With a short roll-out, it should be possible to roll up the slope a little way. The longitudinal ditches end at the foot of the small hill. Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no penetrating supra-regional wind) is likely to come from the north, resulting in a crosswind. Category B solely due to the narrow width of the strips; otherwise it would be Category A. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 10.05.2023. Wies: Site visit on 10 May 2023. The meadow continues to offer a good landing spot, with individual wooden posts at the sides. We have produced a video of the site visit:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 10.09.2023. Wies: Inspection on 10 September 2023. The meadow remains in very good condition.
en\u001fBesichtigung am 10.09.2025, Zust: Viewing on 10 September 2025; condition unchanged. Details: there are pasture fences; the meadow is slightly undulating.
en\u001fBesichtigung am 12.05.2023. Groß: Site visit on 12 May 2023. Large, flat meadow with a mown but short model aeroplane airfield along the north-eastern edge. Windsock on the model aeroplane shed. Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no dominant wider-area wind) from the east. Unfortunately, the meadow is oriented at an angle to the valley axis, meaning it is not possible to land directly into the wind. The nearby meadows near Bramberg are therefore better suited. Site visit video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 12.05.2023. Land: Site visit on 12 May 2023. Landing direction is east to west only. Valley wind (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant regional wind) blows from east to west. Landings are therefore usually with a tailwind. The meadow is initially flat in the eastern part, but rises steeply in the western part. It is also somewhat undulating there. The power line on the southern side (on the left as viewed from the approach) of the meadow poses no problem. When approaching from the side, the meadow behind a rocky outcrop may be obscured or difficult to see. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 12.05.2023. Nord: Site visit on 12 May 2023. The north-eastern meadow remains in very good condition. Slightly sloping from west to east, particularly in the western half, but both landing directions are feasible. Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no dominant regional wind) from the east. Slight cross-slope from north (road) to south (river); not a problem. Low-hanging power line along the small road at the low-lying western end; not a problem. Inspection video: north-eastern meadow, part 1:. Reconnaissance video: north-eastern meadow, part 2:. The south-western meadow is also very good, very long, with a completely unobstructed approach and no longitudinal or transverse gradients. Possibly slightly damp, as it is lower-lying and closer to the river. Reconnaissance video: south-western meadow:.
en\u001fBesichtigung am 12.05.2023. Wies: Site visit on 12 May 2023. The meadow remains in good condition. Flat, with no longitudinal or transverse slopes. Valley wind (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant regional wind) is likely to be predominantly local from the west, as the wind flows into the Pinzgau via the Pass Thurn, situated just to the west. Approach against this wind direction, i.e. from east to west, is clear and unproblematic. In this landing direction (from east to west), the quality is definitely A. An approach from west to east over the marked high-voltage power line, which runs diagonally to the landing direction, is feasible, but only advisable when there is a distinct easterly wind down in the valley. In this landing direction (from west to east), the quality is more likely to be B due to the power line. Site inspection video:

Site visit on 12 May 2023. The meadow remains in very good condition. Slightly sloping from west to east, particularly in the western half, but both landing directions are feasible. Valley wind (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant regional wind) from the east. Slight cross-slope from north (road) to south (river); not a problem. Low-hanging power line along the small road at the low-lying western end; not a problem. Site inspection video:

Site visit on 12 May 2023. The meadow remains in very good condition. Minimal cross-slope, which is not a problem. Valley wind (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant regional wind) from the north. Video of the site visit:
Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadow unchanged. Site visit video, part 1: Site visit video, part 2:

Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadow unchanged. Unobstructed approach from south to north. Large, flat meadow. Unfortunately, it is divided into small sections across the valley axis by paths, ditches and possibly also fences. The area marked on the map is presumably the longest strip running in the direction of the valley, but at just under 200 metres it is very short; hence it is only rated B. This is also where the paragliders’ landing area with a windsock is located. Site inspection video, part 1: Site inspection video, part 2:

Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadow unchanged. Large, flat area, completely unobstructed approach; power lines pose no problem whatsoever. Unfortunately, the fields are divided into small plots running perpendicular to the valley axis; some are used for maize cultivation. The best meadow is probably in the north-eastern corner of the marked area; there is also a paragliding landing site there with a windsock. Site visit video, part 1: Site visit video, part 2:

Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadow unchanged. The largest (north-easternmost) of the marked areas is large and flat. Unobstructed approach towards 33 between the railway line to the west and the high-voltage power line to the east, parallel to and to the left of the high-voltage power line. Power lines are not a problem, but the pylons are dark. Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no dominant regional wind) from the north, meaning a landing into the wind. Site visit video:

Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadows unchanged. All meadows slope noticeably from east to west. Landing direction is therefore only from east to west, towards the village. Valley wind (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant regional wind) from east to west. Landings are therefore usually with a tailwind; however, due to the significant rise in the meadows, landing with a tailwind is not a problem. The northernmost and southernmost meadows are of comparable length and both have unobstructed approaches. The middle, triangular of the three marked meadows is significantly shorter and is more of an emergency landing site. Site inspection video of the northern meadow: Site inspection video of the southern meadow:

Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadows unchanged. Slightly sloping from south to north. Landing possible in both directions. Valley updraft (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant wider-area wind) from the north. The gradient from south to north is so slight that, with a valley wind, landing to the north against the wind is recommended. The strip between the previously marked northern and southern meadows is also suitable for landing. However, this central strip is somewhat bumpier. Power lines pose no problems whatsoever. In terms of quality, given the relatively modest length of each of the three individual meadows, the site rates as a rather poor ‘A’ or a very good ‘B’. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 18.10.2025. Brei: Site visit on 18 October 2025. A wide, flat valley floor with several potential landing sites in the wider vicinity. The marked meadow is suitable, but between the eastern edge of the meadow and the side road branching off the main road in an arc, there is a power line running north-south parallel to the eastern edge of the meadow! An approach from east to west is therefore NOT recommended, or at best would require a longer landing on the fields further to the west, which are, however, much shorter. An approach from west to east into the marked meadow, on the other hand, is perfectly feasible. The recommended landing direction is therefore the 10th. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 18.10.2025. Unve: Inspection on 18 October 2025. Still in very good condition. Both landing directions possible. The field slopes slightly from east to west. The approach from east to west is clearer. Preferred landing direction is therefore from east to west. In this landing direction (from east to west), it is definitely Category A. Landing in the opposite direction (from west to east) is also possible. In this landing direction (from west to east), it is at least a very good B due to the less unobstructed approach. Site visit video:

Site visit on 18 October 2025. Still in very good condition. The meadow slopes gently from west to east. The preferred landing direction is therefore uphill from west to east. Landing in the opposite direction (from east to west) is also possible. A slight cross-slope down towards the road is not a problem. Site inspection video:

Site visit on 18 October 2025. Unchanged as described. The meadow slopes gently from west to east. Approach from west to east is unobstructed. The preferred landing direction is therefore uphill from west to east. In this landing direction (from west to east), it is generally Category A. The approach in the opposite direction (from east to west) is somewhat hampered by trees; in this landing direction, it is therefore only Category B. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 20.06.2026 durch: Inspection on 20 June 2026 by Jan K.: Garmisch West is accessible.
Inspection on 20 June 2026 by Jan K.: New posts in the meadow
en\u001fBesichtigung am 21.12.2024. Eine: Site visit on 21 December 2024. One of numerous excellent landing sites in the Westallgäu’s Pre-Alpine landscape, which is generally very suitable for landing. The fact that this particular meadow has made it into the catalogue is likely to have historical reasons: from 1959 to 1982, this was the gliding site of the Westallgäu Air Sports Group from Lindenberg (source: ), and remained a popular meeting place for model aircraft enthusiasts for many years afterwards. In the 1990s and 2000s, gravel pits were dug into the large meadow. But even these have since been filled in, levelled and restored to meadows, so that by 2024 the meadow will certainly be better (!) than it was during the last inspection in 2003. In fact, an airfield could be set up here again. Tour video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 22.02.2025. Wies: Site visit on 22 February 2025. The meadow remains unchanged as described. Aurach West remains in Category B, as before, due to the incline and the tailwind landing that may consequently be required. Site visit video of the western meadow: (Suggestion: given their different characteristics, separate catalogue entries could be created under the names “Aurach West – Cat B” and “Aurach East – Cat A”. --- Editor’s note: this was implemented on 28 February!)

Inspection on 22 February 2025. Meadow unchanged as described. The eastern meadow, named “Aurach East”, is definitely Category A. Inspection video of the eastern meadow: (Suggestion: given their different characteristics, separate catalogue entries could be created under the names “Aurach West – Cat B” and “Aurach East – Cat A”. --- Editor’s note: this was done on 28 February!)
Inspection on 22 February 2025. Meadows unchanged as described. Inspection video of the north-western meadow: Inspection video of the south-eastern meadow:

Site visit on 22 February 2025. The meadows remained unchanged as described. A mobile pasture fence running east to west divided the meadows roughly in the middle of their length into two halves, as described by Christoph and Thomas. Many horses are grazing all around. The meadows themselves would be Category B; without the mobile pasture fence, they would even be a very good B. However, given the risk that horses might also be standing on the strip of land, Category C is appropriate! Inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 23.04.2023 - Wie: Inspection on 23 April 2023 – Meadows still in very good condition.
en\u001fBesichtigung am 24.10.2024. Mehr: Site visit on 24 October 2024. Several parallel meadows. All meadows slope steeply from south (main road) to north (edge of the forest). Landing is therefore only possible from south to north. The meadows are bordered by a dirt track at the upper, northern end. As described, this can only be driven over by Rumms; however, the meadows are long enough that there is actually no reason to drive that far. Despite the incline, it is classified as Category A due to its ample length and width and the very good surface quality. Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 24.10.2024. Nörd: Site visit on 24 October 2024. The northernmost of the three fields shown on the map. Sloping upwards from west to east. Approach and landing only possible from west to east. The field is bordered on the north side by a row of tall trees. The field is bordered on the south side by a tall group of trees and the floodlight masts of a sports ground. Length 370 metres, width 65 metres. A power line crosses diagonally through the easternmost quarter; however, as the approach is only possible from west to east anyway, this does not cause any interference. The northern field is an acceptable ‘B’ and is therefore the best of the three fields marked on the map in Ellmau. Site inspection video: The westernmost of the three marked fields: Sloping from west to east. Approach and landing only possible from west to east. Approach over a farmstead lying across the field. Total length 480 metres. However, 280 metres after the farmstead to be flown over, the field is divided by a field boundary marked by individual posts, possibly also by a fence. The terrain also becomes noticeably undulating at this point. The western field is a poor B or a very good C and is therefore the second-best of the three fields marked on the map in Ellmau. Inspection video: The southernmost of the three marked fields: A steep cross-slope from a raised embankment along the main road to the south of the field down to the lower-lying golf course to the north of the field. Extremely undulating and hilly. Approach possible from both directions, but obstructed by trees from both directions. Although the total length is 460 metres, it is divided roughly in the middle by a field boundary. It seemed highly doubtful to us that a landing could be achieved here without causing damage. Even for emergency landings, there are more suitable areas in the immediate vicinity, such as the two other fields marked on the map, or even the fairways of the golf course. The southern field should therefore be classified as a poor Category C or even as unlandable. Inspection video: Overall, Ellmau should nevertheless remain in Category B, as the northernmost field in particular (see above) represents an acceptable Category B.
en\u001fBesichtigung am 24.10.2024. Wies: Site visit on 24 October 2024. The meadow remains in good condition. The meadow rises significantly from north-west to south-east, from the lower-lying village up to the edge of the forest. Approach and landing are therefore as shown on the map, from west-north-west to east-south-east. Category B due to the steep climb, but a very good B! Site inspection video:

Site visit on 24 October 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition. This meadow is one of many flat meadows along the Tiroler Ache river, where the valley between St. Johann/Tirol and Kössen is suitable for landing almost all the way along. Video of the site visit:

Site visit on 24 October 2024. Both meadows slope steeply from north to south. Landing is therefore only possible from north to south. Although the approach route runs partly over houses and across the busy main road, this is not a problem at all due to the steep slope of the meadow. The western meadow is now divided into two parallel halves by a new farm track running from north to south. The western half of the western meadow (west of the new farm track) is no longer suitable for landing due to earthworks. The eastern half of the western meadow (east of the new track) remains in very good condition and, despite its width being halved by the new track, is still more than wide enough. Category B due to the steep incline, but a very good B! Site inspection video:
en\u001fBesichtigung am 31.05.2023. Wies: Inspection on 31 May 2023. The meadow remains in very good condition.
en\u001fBesichtigung durch Ralf Paech am: Tour by Ralf Paech on 23 June 2024 (see also his post below). Here is the link to Ralf’s tour video:
en\u001fBetter than Hermagor-East! Watch: Better than Hermagor-East! Watch out for some cables on the westend of the meadow!
en\u001fBin am 10. Mai 2012 in Radstadt: I made an off-field landing in Radstadt on 10 May 2012. The two adjacent main meadows (marked by the red arrow on the SIP) appear to be excellent. However, after landing and walking off the field in the eastern section of the southern runway, I discovered a fixed steel post (approx. 40 cm high)
en\u001fBin am 10.08.25 10 gelandet. Bis: I landed on 10 August 25 at 10. The descriptions given so far are accurate. The north-western section (from the pegs marked on the sketch) is an uneven wet meadow. On the southern side, there are two transverse hollows which should be avoided. However, this is not a problem if you land in the centre. [ WeGlide flight ]
en\u001fBodenhindernisse, Strom-/andere: Obstacles on the ground, power lines and other cables
en\u001fCa. 50 m südlich der früher eing: Approx. 50 m south of the meadow previously marked on the map, a 10 m high telephone line runs in an east-west direction, and a 0.5 m deep stream ditch runs from east to west across the meadow. Under no circumstances should you attempt to drive over this ditch!!!
en\u001fCables in Approach and ditches a: Cables in Approach and ditches at the beginning of the meadow!
en\u001fCross approach is obscured by li: Cross approach is obscured by little hill.
en\u001fDas Feld (westlich Tannheim) kan: The field (west of Tannheim) is best approached from the south and west. To the sides of the markings are former stream beds, which should be avoided when landing. Otherwise, the landing posed no problems. Access is also absolutely straightforward, as a dirt track leads directly from the junction with the main road to the landing field.
en\u001fDas Feld scheint immer noch gut: The field still seems suitable. There is another possible landing site between the power line and the main road. However, there was about 10 cm of snow on the ground when we inspected the site.
en\u001fDie Büsche am Süd-Ost Ende sind: The bushes at the south-eastern end have grown quite tall by now. Please only use this as a last resort!!
en\u001fDie Felder an der Straße entlang: The fields along the road are only moderately suitable. There are lots of posts and a mobile chicken coop that keeps being moved around. The fields to the west are much more suitable.
en\u001fDie Landewiese heißt Löcherberg: The landing field is called Löcherberg, not Löscherberg. The approach is very tight. Category B at most!
en\u001fDie Wiesen in diesem Tal sind im: The meadows in this valley are still there and appear to be in good condition. There are certainly several meadows. Meadows are farmed; cows and agricultural machinery may be left there.
en\u001fDie einstige Wiese Vorderriss is: The former Vorderriss meadow has (as of 2019) been converted into a storage area for long timber and is bordered on both sides by rows of posts several metres high and as thick as tree trunks. Absolutely UNPASSABLE.
en\u001fDie in der SIP dargestellte west: The smaller meadow shown on the SIP, situated further to the west with an approach direction of 10, is NOT suitable for landing. The larger meadow shown in the SIP, situated further to the east with an approach from the north-west, lies exactly on the line connecting the sawmill down in the valley and the church up in the village. The left-hand boundary of the field is marked along its entire length by a light-coloured, tarmacked track; its south-eastern end therefore marks the start of the field. Approach: only from the north-west, not too low over the sawmill towards the church and clearly up the slope. Fly over! Our assessment: Due to the steep gradient, which necessitates a genuine uphill landing, and because of the unpleasant crosswinds, this is clearly only a B minus. You should not plan to use this meadow; it merely safeguards the otherwise unlandable Lesachtal valley against unexpected, massive sink holes.
en\u001fDie markierten Wiesen liegen que: The marked meadows lie across the valley; an approach from the directions indicated is not possible! Between the first and second meadows (viewed from the south), a power line runs across the valley (Figure 2). This makes an approach in the direction of the valley difficult. The main problem, however, is that the fields running along the valley are too short, and a landing would have to be made across several fields with varying vegetation, ditches and terraces. The field slightly to the north (Figure 3) is better situated, but it also has a power line and posts that are difficult to see.
en\u001fDie optimale Landerichtung wäre: The best direction for landing would be towards the church / east. On the day I took the photograph of the meadow, there was a tractor working there, which certainly becomes an obstacle when it’s there mowing. Running parallel to the road towards the village centre, the meadow is divided into strips about 30 metres wide by wooden posts.
en\u001fDifficult Approach between house: Difficult Approach between houses and cables!
en\u001fDo not land when used as a cow-m: Do not land when used as a cow-meadow! Narrow valley!
en\u001fDo not roll over the path! Use w: Do not roll over the path! Use west part, east part is too bumpy!
en\u001fDteep Landings uphill! Prefer ea: Dteep Landings uphill! Prefer east field, better choice: Achenkirch!
en\u001fE Remonte vers E. Atterro face a: Head east. You must land facing east. Choose your landing spot depending on the crops.
E/W Prairie. Suitable for various crops. Can be positioned either way.
en\u001fE/W Reco a pied indispensable. P: E/W Reconnaissance on foot is essential. Meadow.
en\u001fEasier to approach from the east: Easier to approach from the east
en\u001fEbener Talboden. Taleinwind (an: Flat valley floor. Valley wind (on days with thermals but no wider regional wind) from the north.
en\u001fEine von mehreren Landemöglichke: One of several landing options near Saalfelden
en\u001fEine von mehreren unproblematisc: One of several unproblematic country meadows near Saalfelden. Approach from west to east; in the approach area there is an industrial estate and a clearly visible hill in the foreground of the meadow.
en\u001fEine weitere Aussenlandewiese (g: Another outfield landing area (yellow) near Ehrwald; currently well mown and better than the other published fields as it lacks boundary posts. There is a ditch at the start of the field, followed by a bump after the first 50 metres, so ideally you should touch down shortly after the ditch. The approach is clear and unproblematic. Strong crosswind blowing in from the valley. VGL.
en\u001fEnges Tal, keine normale Platzru: Narrow valley; a normal traffic pattern is not possible. A rollable bump in the field.
en\u001fFSW Landewiesentour: alles ok, w: FSW Landewiesen Tour: everything’s fine, just as described

FSW Landewiesentour: everything’s fine, as described. Landing is only possible in the second half of the meadow, where the ground also slopes slightly upwards. In the first half, there are bumps in the ground and markers that are difficult to see; it is also marshy in places. When approaching, watch out for the hill in front of the swimming lake (approx. 120m above sea level).
FSW Landewiese Tour: everything’s fine, as described. Be sure to avoid the north-western part of the meadow due to bumps and a dip in the ground
en\u001fFSW Landewiesentour: alles ok. A: FSW Landewiesentour: everything’s fine. Approach possible from both directions. The northernmost strip of meadow slopes slightly. Watch out for vegetation and stones
en\u001fFSW Landewiesentour: alles ok. K: FSW Landewiesentour: everything OK. Küb East: several strips of meadow, varying vegetation, marked out with pegs. A long final approach is not possible due to the hill situated very close to the meadow. Küb West: several strips of meadow, varying gradients and vegetation. The field on the roadside in the eastern half of the field and the central field in the western half have the gentlest slopes. Approach possible from both directions
en\u001fFSW Landewiesentour: wie beschri: FSW Landewiesentour: as described. The Mürzzuschlag meadow is definitely the best choice!
en\u001fFahre häufig an der Wiese vorbei: I often cycle past the meadow: Still very good.
en\u001fFeld immer noch landbar, wie ein: The field is still accessible, as shown on the map and in the photographs. Watch out for vegetation and pegs where necessary. There is currently a manure heap at the north-eastern end (as of July 2021), but this is not a hindrance. There is a windsock belonging to the paragliding school near the Alpspitzbahn car park.
en\u001fFeldweg problemlos überrollbar,: The dirt track is easily passable; the field to the south of the track is also accessible – take care of the barn
en\u001fFence at the beginning of the me: Fence at the beginning of the meadow!
en\u001fFence posts, you can roll over t: Fence posts, you can roll over the path
en\u001fField is framed by clearly visib: Field is framed by clearly visible obstacles (roads, fences). Only one landing direction available. Landing is slightly uphill. Final approach is exactly over a chimney. Meadow. Length 350m.
en\u001fField is no longer usable becaus: Field is no longer usable because half of it is planted with fruit trees!
en\u001fField is on the lake side very u: Field is on the lake side very uneven!
en\u001fFläche wird wohl nicht mehr als: This area is probably no longer used as a timber yard, and only half of it is still used as a car park (the area marked in red in the picture). However, there are still a few scattered posts, as thick as tree trunks, remaining from the former timber yard. At the western end of the area marked in yellow in the image, these posts have been cut back to a height of approximately 1–1.5 metres, so that the approach is now reasonably clear again. There is also a low building at the south-western corner. At the eastern end of the area marked in yellow, there is a single, similarly shortened tree-trunk post and a wire fence. In my view, this should again be categorised as C.
en\u001fFreier Anflug 16/34. Im nördlich: Unobstructed approach to 16/34. Metal posts in the northern section, hence a staggered threshold at 16. Watch out for masts and cranes when approaching from the side.
en\u001fFreier Anflug 17/35. Wiese recht: Unobstructed approach 17/35. The meadow is quite uneven. Cattle are likely to be grazing there. Consider a diagonal landing; this will extend the landing run to approx. 300 m. To the east of the river/road is a model aeroplane airfield, but the meadows are separated by metal pasture fences.
en\u001fFreier Anflug 24 auf ansteigende: Free approach 24 over an undulating meadow. Due to undulations in the meadow, keep more towards the road (on the right). Another option is further to the left, across the road and to the left of the flags. Cattle are likely to be grazing!
en\u001fFreier Anflug 26. Hochspannung i: Free approach 26. High voltage during the crosswind approach (east side of the valley). Power line with a pylon at the end of the meadow (just before the road). Pasture fence in the touchdown area! Likely to be grazing livestock!
en\u001fFreier Anflug 33, nur linker Que: Free approach 33, only a left-hand cross-wind approach is possible. (High-voltage power lines!). Stay to the left of the power lines. Wooden posts in the middle of the field!
en\u001fFrom east free approach, but sli: From east free approach, but slightly sloping.
en\u001fGefälle vom Wald zur Ortschaft h: A slope leading down from the woods towards the village. If possible, approach via the village
en\u001fGelandet am 21.6.2026. Schön gem: Landed on 21 June 2026. The grass was nicely mown; a bit bumpier than usual at gliding airfields, but not far off. The windsock next to the runway is quite handy. A little closer to the farm there’s another field – wider and shorter. No cows or other animals that day. No people to be seen anywhere either. [ WeGlide flight ]
A good landing field, as described. Landing direction: west. The field slopes upwards towards the west. High-voltage power lines are clearly visible. Very friendly people in the area. There is a small restaurant nearby.
en\u001fGute, von beiden Seiten hinderni: A good meadow that can be approached without any obstacles from either side.
en\u001fGuter aber kleiner Acker, der zu: A good but small field, which has been freshly ploughed and rolled as of today (25 June 2022). There are a great many trees on approach; you should only touch down once you’re in the ploughed area. The approach could be quite steep and challenging even with much better aircraft than the ASK21, as the trees are quite tall. Furthermore, the field slopes down slightly again in the final (western) section. The field is currently used for growing grass (as of June 2022) and the owners live in the farmhouse directly to the west.
en\u001fHab keine Möglichkeit, die Länge: I’ve got no way of measuring the length. Estimated minimum 400 metres. Good approach path.
en\u001fHabe heute 15.03.2025 die Wiese: I went to have a look at the meadow today, 15 March 2025. Everything is as previously described. That said, the alternative near the motorway slopes slightly towards the motorway, and you end up on a slightly sloping track. I also checked out the next field to the north. I think that one is better as it’s less hilly. Similarly, to the south, before the road, there are flatter fields where, if necessary, you could land facing west or east, depending on which way the wind is blowing. Watch out for power lines.
en\u001fHeute mit der Drohne in der nähe: I was out in the area with the drone today and took a few shots to show that the landing pad is still in good condition. It’s still very easy to land on and in good condition.
en\u001fHigh trees and 2 Cables on the e: High trees and 2 Cables on the east side! Railway-bank and cable which you can roll under on the west side!
en\u001fHouse with antenna mast at the s: House with antenna mast at the southeast end
en\u001fIch bin am 31.5.03 im Rahmen des: I landed here on 31 May 2003 as part of the AAC and thought this field was the best place to land; even though the approach takes you past a house, it’s long enough. The field lies alongside a small track and next to a farm with a paddock. There are probably other fields suitable for landing as well. However, it should be noted that there are quite a few small power lines in this valley. Furthermore, the vegetation consisted of young maize, which is unlikely to make landing particularly pleasant in summer!
en\u001fIch bin dort am 16.04.2025 gelan: I landed there on 16 April 2025. The field is very large and very flat. There was no vegetation, just brown earth; the ground was relatively firm. The village is very close by.
en\u001fIch bin in östliche Richtung gel: I ended up heading east. The meadow was very flat, partly mown, with loooads of space.
en\u001fIch habe mir den Platz in Zug ma: I’ve had a look at the site in Zug and would describe it as an EMERGENCY LANDING AREA.
en\u001fIch wohne 10 km entfernt und war: I live 10 km away and cycled there. I also know the place as a glider pilot. It’s perfect for off-field landings! Best wishes, Marco
en\u001fIm Herbst 2021 immer noch gut la: Still easy to land in autumn 2021.
en\u001fIm Inntal gibt es verschiedene L: There are various landing sites in the Inn Valley, but the somewhat limited options are primarily due to Innsbruck’s airspace restrictions. This landing site is used exclusively for skydiving. Please note that, whilst taller vegetation is generally not a problem for the high-wing aircraft used for drop-offs, the situation may be different for a glider.
en\u001fIm Norden des Landefeldes steht: To the north of the airfield, there has been a large petrol station with a lorry workshop and lorry yard by the road for at least three years. For this reason, the meadow shown in SIP has become smaller. However, it is still sufficient. The issue of the ditches must be taken seriously. These are not irrigation ditches that you might be able to drive over – they are concrete channels overgrown with grass.!!!
en\u001fImmer noch eine hervorragende La: It’s still an excellent landing area, although the grass is a bit long at the moment. You just need to open and hold the barriers for the retriever; these are located on the access road. The first one is a little to the south of the field on the dirt track, and the second one is, if you follow the dirt track westwards, right by the small village of Fall.
en\u001fIn Mathon bis Ende Saison (Föhn: In Mathon, until the end of the season (Föhn winds and so on), restrictions apply as shown in the picture. Someone is building a new barn on the slope and had to carry out slope stabilisation work; as a result, the excavated material is being dumped on the meadow, shortening its length. The condition, however, is that once the structural work is complete, he must clear the material away and restore the meadow. This should, however, be sorted by the start of the new season.
en\u001fIn der Wiese sind vermehrt orang: There are an increasing number of orange poles in the meadow. Farchant North is clearly better suited for landing.
en\u001fIn the approach from east high v: In the approach from east high voltage line!
en\u001fIn the meadow on the south side: In the meadow on the south side are the paths overrollable
en\u001fIn the middle a circular wall wi: In the middle a circular wall with 30m diameter and 1m height
en\u001fIn the north the field is limite: In the north the field is limited by a ditch. In the east the field is bordered by a cycle track. In the south, the field is bordered by a high voltage power line. The final approach is only free in landing direction south. The field is not inclined. Land parallel to the cycle track in southern direction. Meadow. Total length 370m from ditch to power line.

Feedback:
- 26 August 2025, Jonas Blahnik: During my site visit in mid-August, there were many bales of straw on both meadows – please note! Without these bales, the northern meadow remains a good option.
- 1 September 2023, Daniel Bauer: Northern meadow is suitable for landing; high-voltage power line to the west of the runway
- 1 December 2021, Jan Lyczywek: Inspection on 9 September 2021. Two fields: 1. Field north of the motorway: quite good. Very firm, level ground. When approaching from west to east (landing direction 07), a high-voltage power line runs diagonally across the approach path, but can be flown over. 2. Meadow south of the motorway: Caution! Two parallel, tall high-voltage power lines directly on the western edge of the field, which are likely to make an approach from west to east impossible. The field south of the motorway is therefore likely only suitable for landing from east to west (landing direction 25). Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no strong, wide-ranging winds) from the east. Site inspection video:
- 12 April 2021, Roland Bieri: The site visit took place on 10 April 2019; it is a relatively good field. Other fields previously documented as landing options are significantly worse

Direction: 17/35
Inspected: 2022

Feedback:
- 21 April 2022, Walter Krebs: Unchanged. Due to the rows of trees on both sides of the field, the actual available length is limited. A clearly visible prison, surrounded by high walls, has been built slightly south of the landing field. en\u001fInfo: ATTN: RWY08 with north/nor:
Information: ATTN: RWY08 with north/northeast wind lee effect due to landfill mountain. Usable in direction 08 from threshold approx. 500 m. Pay attention to uneven ground, especially after rain the field can be very soft! TO/LDG on the south side of the runway, stay on or close to the tarmac strip. LDG 26 with MOT only as a long landing to the hangar. When approaching from the north, please strictly avoid the villages of Hopfen (Kurklinik) and Eschach. Avoid the residential area on departure 08, keep left towards the Festspielhaus. On the cross take-off for motorised aircraft, take off to the middle of Forggensee.

en\u001fInfo: Always heavy wind from eas:
Information: Always heavy wind from east
Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 15/-
Tour: 2020
Amended: 16.03.2021
Reported hazards: uneven ground, electrical and other cables
Feedback:
- 15 March 2021, Gerhard Sindermann: I landed there without any problems on 30 July 2020 with my Ka6 CR; see the flight path. The field is fenced off; the farmer wasn’t too keen at first, as the grass was due to be mown very soon. However, he helped me and didn’t cause any trouble. If you land on this field, try to land as far to the right as possible; that way, it’s not far to the exit. Unfortunately, I landed right in the middle. As the meadow is fenced off, there could be animals in it! Length 200m; apart from the fence, there are no obstacles on the approach. The power lines run parallel to the meadows and do not pose an obstacle. It’s also possible to land beyond the fence; there’s a model aeroplane airfield there with a small hut and a windsock, and to the left of that is a wide meadow. You can see everything clearly and measure it out on Google Maps. There are certainly several options; I do not consider the option marked further south on the previous map to be very good. There, you would have to land across the fields. A landing direction of SSE, i.e. out of the valley, is advisable due to the valley wind. Easy access via the Dammweg. On Google Maps (Street View, heading north – you’re looking directly down the approach path), you can see clearly from the road to the left into the grounds; however, these are the meadows that run across the landscape. If you drive as far as just before the petrol station, you’ll see the little cottage at the model aeroplane airfield on the left-hand side of the grounds. Nevertheless, it provides a good insight into the landing conditions.
- 15 March 2021, Jan Lyczywek: A small, narrow model aeroplane field, but with a perfectly maintained lawn and situated in the direction of the valley (wind direction). On days with thermals and no regional wind, the wind blows into the valley from the south. However, a regional northerly wind (northerly foehn) can reach right down to the ground!
- 26 March 2011, Thomas Keuschnig: initial feedback. Pictures from the southern ‘Alternativwiese’!
- 12 March 2004, Thomas Keuschnig: a very large area, but please note that this is the only meadow without a power line or fence posts. There is generally a strong easterly wind, so land away from the valley. The meadow is bordered by a fence at both ends. It is easily recognisable from the air by the distinctive parallel features: the ditch on one side and the power line on the other.

The owner and the tenant are noticeably and understandably annoyed by the numerous paraglider landings (and presumably even landings by microlights?!). During our conversation, we tried to explain the difference between such landings ‘for fun’ and our emergency landings, which, of course, nobody does for fun. The owner urged us to include a note here stating that she and her tenant do not wish to have any landings on their property. She also asks that people refrain from frequent visits to her property, as it has apparently already happened that cars have turned round in the tall grass or even driven into the meadow to have a look around! This is very handy for landing, as it means you can fly into the wind but still head up the valley (!) and have an unobstructed approach to the meadow, just as Holger has already described. - 18 August 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Landing on 18 August 2023. The meadow is back to its original, excellent condition. - 31 August 2022, Holger Fuhr: In Mathon, until the end of the season (Föhn winds and so on), restrictions apply as shown in the picture. Someone is building a new barn on the slope and had to carry out slope stabilisation work; as a result, the excavated material is being dumped on the meadow, shortening its length. The condition, however, is that once the structural work is complete, he must clear the material away and restore the meadow. This should, however, be sorted by the start of the new season.
