Tilt: Strongly ascending
Tour: 2023
Amended: 14.05.2023
Feedback:
- 12 May 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadows unchanged. All meadows slope noticeably from east to west. Landing direction is therefore only from east to west, towards the village. Valley wind (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant regional wind) from east to west. Landings are therefore usually with a tailwind; however, due to the significant rise in the meadows, landing with a tailwind is not a problem. The northernmost and southernmost meadows are of comparable length and both have unobstructed approaches. The middle, triangular of the three marked meadows is significantly shorter and is more of an emergency landing site. Site inspection video of the northern meadow: Site inspection video of the southern meadow:
- 15 March 2021, Königsdorf: Free approach 24 over an undulating meadow. Due to undulations in the meadow, keep more towards the road (on the right). Another option is further to the left, across the road and to the left of the flags. Cattle are likely to be grazing!
- 27.03.2011, ???: initial feedback
- 27.09.2003, ???: Rises slightly towards the west!

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: -/25
Tour: 2023
Amended: 17.05.2023
Feedback:
- 16 May 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadow unchanged. The largest (north-easternmost) of the marked areas is large and flat. Unobstructed approach towards 33 between the railway line to the west and the high-voltage power line to the east, parallel to and to the left of the high-voltage power line. Power lines are not a problem, but the pylons are dark. Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no dominant regional wind) from the north, meaning a landing into the wind. Site visit video:
- 15 March 2021, Königsdorf: Free approach 33, only a left-hand cross-wind approach is possible. (High-voltage power lines!). Stay to the left of the power lines. Wooden posts in the middle of the field!
- 15 March 2021, Manfred Schwegler: Approach 25 – over high-voltage power lines and houses (may be critical due to poor flap effect)! The meadow rises steeply towards the west. The valley is somewhat narrow. Valley wind often blows from the north at right angles to the landing direction (in the absence of wider-area winds). A few pegs at the edge. Adjoining former SIP fields are partly dotted with pegs. Alternative: ID 228 Aschau Zillertal (500 m to the north).
- 27 March 2011, Ulrich Plarre: initial feedback

Surface: Grassland
Direction: -/25
Inspection: 2023
Updated: 19 March 2023
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground, power lines/other cables
Feedback:
- 22 February 2023, Hanspeter Schmid: The meadows are approximately 350 m long in total and level. An approach from the west is only feasible in strong headwinds and with very effective flaps, due to the high-voltage power line running down the valley side. The final section, at an altitude of at least 100 m immediately at the start of the meadow, requires an extremely steep approach! I would approach from the east; the valley is wide enough for a ‘southern circuit’ and one should touch down early enough to fly under the power line running across the meadow. The pylons are spaced approx. 40–50 m apart and are approx. 7–8 m high; the cables sag to a height of approx. 5 m. The touchdown point is level with the two huts; one of them has a new roof covering as of 2020 (Google Maps). The roll-out distance is also long enough for tailwind landings (which will probably be the case most of the time). In the southern third, there are plastic or reinforced concrete marker posts with white flags attached; therefore, the second pole spacing from the Vorderrhein would be the ideal line to ensure sufficient distance from the markers. I would definitely prefer these meadows to the landing fields near Disentis, even if landing between the poles gives you a bit of a thrill.
- 15 October 2003, Christian Holzner: Highly recommended! Best approached from the east. A power line crosses the eastern part. The northern boundary is marked by a high-voltage power line.

Feedback:
- 28.07.2023, Alistair Bruce: The easterly option visited in July 2023 in an LS4. A good alternative to the main marked field. A fence is at the western end and a diagonal trench, both visible from the air. A path splits the field in two segments, this could be safely rolled over if a long landing is made. An approach in both directions possible. Video of landing:
- 26 July 2023, Alistair Bruce: Still a good option
- 12 May 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 12 May 2023. The meadow remains in good condition. Flat, with no longitudinal or transverse slopes. Valley wind (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant regional wind) is likely to be predominantly local from the west, as the wind flows into the Pinzgau via the Pass Thurn, situated just to the west. Approach against this wind direction, i.e. from east to west, is clear and unproblematic. In this landing direction (from east to west), the quality is definitely A. An approach from west to east over the marked high-voltage power line, which runs diagonally to the landing direction, is feasible, but only advisable when there is a distinct easterly wind down in the valley. In this landing direction (from west to east), the quality is more likely to be B due to the power line. Site inspection video:
- 15 March 2021, Benjamin Bachmaier: An Eastern Alternative

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: -/27
Tilt: Ascending
Tour: 2024
Amended: 03.11.2024
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground
Feedback:
- 3 November 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 2 November 2024. Meadow sloping upwards from the east (village) to the west (edge of the woodland on the hillside). Landing is therefore only possible from east to west. Unobstructed approach; a very long final approach across the entire valley is possible. Approach runs between a residential area of detached houses to the north and a farm to the south. The steady climb presents no difficulties on landing, but keeps the roll-out distance short. Slight cross-slope from north (higher) to south (lower); not a problem. Slight undulations in the ground; not a problem. A few clearly visible marker posts along a field boundary in a direct westerly extension of the farm; not a problem. Quality A due to the unobstructed approach and the helpful climb. Site inspection video:

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: -/27
Tilt: Slightly ascending
Tour: 2021
Amended: 30.11.2021
Reported hazards: uneven ground
Feedback:
- 30 November 2021, Jan Lyczywek: Landing on 12 August 2021. Meadow slopes from east to west. Landing is therefore only possible from east to west (landing direction 27). Meadow is partly undulating. Cross-slope from south to north. Valley inflow (on days with consistent thermals and no significant cross-regional wind) in the Achensee valley from the north. In the Pertisau side valley, the valley inflow presumably flows round the foot of the Seebergspitze and then continues up the side valley from the east (?). The valley looks narrow, but there is room for a standard traffic pattern: it makes sense to make the final approach on the southern edge of the village = the northern slope of the Zwölferkopf – this is the last chance for a slope wind; watch out for cable cars. Crosswind approach heading north, parallel to the shore above the lake. Final approach heading west over the houses on the northern edge of the village. Alternatives: the meadow further west (west of the westernmost houses) looks larger from the air, but is divided into very small sections. The meadows on the southern edge of the village – the northern slope of the Zwölferkopf – are also divided into sections. Flight:

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: -/28
Tour: 2023
Amended: 27.06.2023
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground, power cables and other cables
Feedback:
- 19.06.2023, Ladislav Jozsa: At the time of the landing possibly the only meadow with cut grass. Wind was pretty much calm. You have to land out towards the village. The slope of the terrain is upwards. Make sure you have 10km/h landing speed more than normal and aim for the very beginning of the meadow, just past the road south of the meadow. It's not an easy landing - you have a road and houses 200m in front of you - but uphill which is more steep towards the road in the north. Try to not take this field in account unless you have to, there's a significant risk of damaging your glider. There are very nice and helpful people around. The vice mayor house is just around the corner and he helped me a lot while I was waiting for transport back. [ WeGlide Flug ]
- 28 December 2021, Nikolai Wolf: - It still looks alright. - The only meadow in the valley. - A high-voltage power line to the south and a telephone line to the north. - The road in the valley is very, very narrow – in my opinion, it’s only accessible from the north-west with a trailer – the roads through the villages from the south-east are rather too narrow.
- 26 March 2011, Jan Lyczywek: The smaller meadow shown on the SIP, situated further to the west with an approach direction of 10, is NOT suitable for landing. The larger meadow shown in the SIP, situated further to the east with an approach from the north-west, lies exactly on the line connecting the sawmill down in the valley and the church up in the village. The left-hand boundary of the field is marked along its entire length by a light-coloured, tarmacked track; its south-eastern end therefore marks the start of the field. Approach: only from the north-west, not too low over the sawmill towards the church and clearly up the slope. Fly over! Our assessment: Due to the steep gradient, which necessitates a genuine uphill landing, and because of the unpleasant crosswinds, this is clearly only a B minus. You should not plan to use this meadow; it merely safeguards the otherwise unlandable Lesachtal valley against unexpected, massive sink holes.
- 24 February 2004, Herwig Wagner: The Lesachtal is known far beyond Carinthia as an unlandable area riddled with ropes. The Carinthians say that every second glider that lands outside the Lesachtal is written off afterwards. The only – albeit not entirely straightforward – landing option!

Feedback:
- 9 December 2025, Urs Lange: Landed on 11 September 2025, having inspected the site beforehand. Large meadow, slightly undulating with rising terrain. Approach from the north-west. Overall, very good for landing. However, please note the following when retrieving the glider: - It is impossible to access the site from the N94 by passing under the bridge and through the woods to the north towards the meadow with a trailer (tight bends, road barely visible)! - You must approach from the north via the village of Pra Périer! [ WeGlide flight ]
- 13 March 2025, Mathias Schunk: Reconnaissance on 12 March 2025. Field remains in very good condition.
- 25 March 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Inspected on 24 March 2023. Field still in very good condition. Inspection video part 1: Inspection video part 2:
- 2 April 2022, Jürgen Wisbacher: Inspected on 30 March 2022. The field is a pasture situated at an altitude of approximately 860. Easily approachable and suitable for landing from the direction of the lake. Landing direction: NW. The field is enclosed by an electric pasture fence. Presumably usable in spring; from May onwards, cows are likely to be on the meadow. The first 100 m of the meadow are uneven and slope more steeply, so they cannot be used. However, this section is clearly distinguishable and marked by a transverse ridge (2nd picture). After that, approximately 500 m are available for landing (1st picture). In addition to this meadow, another meadow on the plateau further up to the right is also suitable for landing. However, to reach it, you must first fly over a 5-metre-high power line running across the area.

Feedback:
- 21 March 2021, Christopher Hanson: There is a stream (ditch) on the western edge, though it is clearly visible despite the vegetation on the banks; the terrain slopes from east to west (approx. 5%). When approaching from direction 19, there is a power line, but it should be far enough away. At the northern end, there is a distinct mound of earth (see photos). The western field, which borders the north-south road, is relatively bumpy and a power line runs directly over it! The northern fields are relatively steep, strewn with stones and likely to have animals on them. In my opinion, this is the only field or meadow suitable for landing amongst those available there. I landed there on 25 August 2020, heading 19; the landing and roll-out went smoothly. The stream made packing up a bit of a challenge (see photo), but it was manageable, and the local farmer’s staff were very helpful.

Surface: Grassland
Direction: 02/20
Inspection: 2026
Amended: 3 July 2026
Feedback:
- 27 May 2026, Beat Gassmann: Off-field landing on 27 May 2026. Very tall vegetation across the entire site (waist-high grass). Several concrete posts mark the boundaries. These are practically invisible when the vegetation is high. However, there are large gaps between the posts. There is sufficient space even for large aircraft. Please note: The entire site slopes gently down towards the lake in a south-easterly direction. See the yellow hatching. At the position of the aircraft shown on the map, the lateral slope is at its steepest – a proper hollow. That is exactly where I came to a halt with my 15-metre wing. See photos. The first choice is the area east of the posts. It is level here and can be approached from both directions. The second choice is the area between the middle and eastern rows of posts. Here, you should keep closer to the eastern side, as there is less lateral slope here and you can come to a halt south of the hollow. The third choice is an approach from the south-east towards the north-west. Slightly uphill, but no lateral slope. Probably the best choice in strong winds, but also the greatest danger due to the posts. Wind: In the event of an early landing (before 13:30), thermal winds are negligible. Therefore, an approach across the lake towards the north is recommended due to the rising terrain. The later in the afternoon and the more thermally active the air mass, the stronger the wind from the west/south-west becomes. In this case, consider the approach via the motorway towards the south or the third option. Conclusion: A very large hay meadow. No cows, but tall grass is to be expected in May. The posts are a nuisance; they are barely visible when the vegetation is high but can be managed, as they are static boundary markers. The position of the posts can be estimated. You can make out the boundary line by the vegetation. In addition, the large tree to the east of the fields and the embankment on the lakeshore serve as markers to help estimate the position. Ground conditions are excellent. No ditches.

Surface: Grassland
Direction: 04/22
Gradient: Slightly ascending
Inspected: 2023
Updated: 26 March 2023
Reported hazards: uneven ground
Feedback:
- 27 June 2023, Thomas Reinhold: Low vegetation on 27 June 2023. However, an irrigation reel is already lying in wait at the side.
- 25 March 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Inspection on 24 March 2023. Meadow area divided into two halves by a ditch. The eastern half is lower-lying and flat; the western half rises steeply from the ditch towards the west. Approach from east to west (out of the valley): Glide-in over the flat eastern half of the meadow. Touchdown after the ditch, roll-out uphill. Longest roll-out distance and steepest climb along the diagonal. In this approach direction, the meadow is a ‘good’ C-meadow or perhaps even a poor B. Approach from west to east (valley-inward): Glide in along the elongated farm buildings, as low as possible over the western half of the meadow, which slopes steeply in this approach direction from west to east. Touch down immediately after the ditch, roll-out on the flat and not very long eastern half. This approach direction is likely only feasible with a strong easterly wind blowing out of the valley. In this approach direction, the meadow is definitely only a C. Reconnaissance video part 1 (filmed from the western end):. Reconnaissance video part 2 (filmed from the eastern end):

- 22 March 2026, Simeon Schmauß: During the site visit in March 2026, a 100 m long and approximately 3 m deep hole was discovered in the centre of the meadow, dividing it into two separate areas. In addition, at the northern end, approximately 200 metres of the meadow has been excavated at a gentle slope as part of the river restoration work. To the south, the meadow is bordered by the embankment of the new cycle path; to the north, it drops steeply down to the river. This leaves only two flat areas, each approximately 150 metres long and no more than 30 metres wide, which is why this meadow should only be used in the very worst-case scenario. The approach from the north-east is free of obstacles and is preferable, partly due to the gently sloping edge of the excavation – however, should the pilot overshoot the end of the first meadow, the aircraft would slide into the 3-metre-deep hole. Aerial photograph: Simeon Schmauß; ground photographs: Michael Watzke

Surface: Grass Land

Tour: 2024
Amended: 13.08.2024
Reported hazards: Obstacles on the ground, power lines and other cables
Feedback:
- 9 July 2024, Benedikt Bursian: Landed on 9 July 2024 north of the power line marked north of Field 1. The meadow rises slightly towards the west; there is a fence post in the centre of the meadow. The eastern part of Field 1 is divided into smaller fields, which are marked with (possibly variable) fence posts. Take care to avoid the power lines already marked on the map when approaching.
- 15 March 2021, Manfred Schwegler: Two meadows (1) and (2). Be aware of valley winds; when there are thermals but no wider regional wind, the wind is usually from the north. Watch out for numerous high-voltage power lines, huts, isolated stakes and livestock. 1. South-western meadow, 350 m: When approaching from 07, touch down only after the second row of huts. When approaching from 25, watch out for the high-voltage power line crossing the approach path and, behind the bypass road, touch down only after the cross-path. Pictures from different years show, in some cases, varying use/layout of the meadow (possibly areas with pasture fencing). Choose a suitable landing area. 2. North-eastern meadow, 200 m: A safe approach is only possible from direction 25, via the small crossroad. Take care to avoid the pasture fencing and street lamps on the crossroad! Due to a high-voltage power line running across the south-western part of the meadow, an approach towards 07 would be problematic! (Use Meadow 1 for a landing on 07.)
- 17 February 2021, Christoph Dolainsky: New Scharnitz bypass runs right through the old field
- 17 February 2021, Christoph Dolainsky: New Scharnitz bypass runs right through the old field

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 08/-
Tour: 2023
Amended: 14.05.2023
Reported hazards: Electricity and other cables
Feedback:
- 14 May 2023, Johannes Schwemmer: Visit by car on 12 May 2023 during the Unterwössen cross-country flying week. Meadow Plan A (blue) is easy to approach from the west (straight) or south (90-degree landing turn). Video showing the current condition here; it’s slightly uphill but appears to pose no problems. The alternative field (yellow) is shorter but usable. When approaching from the east, it’s best to fly parallel to the power line, which runs along the southern edge of the field, and land in the centre. Watch out for the new streetlights on approach.

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 08/-
Tour: 2024
Amended: 08.11.2024
Reported hazards: Electricity and other cables
Feedback:
- 3 November 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 3 November 2024. Unchanged as described. A cul-de-sac with three street lamps divides the meadow into a western and an eastern half. The western half of the meadow slopes upwards from west to east towards the cul-de-sac. Approach to the western half is therefore only possible from west to east. The western half of the meadow is divided transversely by several field boundaries; there are individual marker posts here. The eastern half of the meadow has no longitudinal slope. However, access to the eastern half is still only possible from west to east, as a power line crosses at the eastern end. Access to the eastern half would have to be made flying low over the cul-de-sac and between the street lamps; the distance between the street lamps is more than ample for this. Site inspection video:
- 3 March 2021, Christian Gerritzen: Attached are some photos from 2016
- 28 February 2021, Konrad Mahlknecht: All the information is still up to date.
- 11 October 2003, Arno Hutter: You can land right next to the main road, and if that doesn’t work out, you can also go down the small side street (behind the lamppost).

- 15 August 2023, Klaus-Dieter Degen: Maize is currently being grown on the northern meadow, so it is no longer accessible. The southern meadow remains accessible.
- 29 April 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Drove past (but did not stop) on 28 April 2023. The northern field (north of the B 178) is still ploughed up. The southern meadow (south of the B 178 main road) is somewhat shorter, but still grade A. It slopes upwards from west to east, so the landing is from west to east. Numerous even better meadows can be found in the Tiroler Ache valley, both to the south towards Kirchdorf and to the north in the Hagertal.
- 14 December 2022, Klaus-Dieter Degen: The northern meadow has currently been ploughed (December 2022). Information regarding its future use in spring 2023 is strongly recommended!

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 09/27
Tour: 2021
Amended: 17.07.2021
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground
Feedback:
- 7 May 2021, Alexander Keupp: A narrow strip between a row of trees and the houses. When approaching from the west, there are tall trees on the left-hand side. On the right, near the last houses (towards the west), there are posts. Flat meadow ground, possibly wet (depression). Posts near the last house towards the west.
- 27.03.2011, Hugo Gold: initial feedback
- 13.10.2003, Hugo Gold: Situated at the western end of the inaccessible Lesachtal valley, this meadow (marked in green) can be approached easily from both sides. It is located south of the hang-gliding and paragliding landing site (marked in yellow), which is equipped with a windsock. The ground is relatively flat and soft, and the meadow is also well suited to heavier aircraft. In the middle of the meadow, to the north and south, there are two small but clearly visible pillars (blue circles), which do not, however, obstruct landing. The obstacles are approximately 40 metres apart, so they pose a problem at most for the Open Class. It is best to touch down in the eastern half. The slopes to the north of this are very thermally active (hence the paragliders) and still offer a good chance of gaining altitude from a low height. Landing at the paragliding site is not normally recommended, as there are too many people standing around there. Purple arrow: best approach to the slope.

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 09/27
Tour: 2024
Amended: 08.07.2024
Reported hazards: uneven ground
Feedback:
- 8 July 2024, Uwe Ernst: Landing on 5 July 2024. The field remained in very good condition. Good landing conditions. No damage to the field caused during landing. Removing the aircraft proved extremely difficult, as the farmer had blocked all access routes. No willingness whatsoever to find a mutual solution. The police, who were called, did not consider the matter to fall within their remit. [ WeGlide flight ]
- 13.05.2023, Jan Zummach: Still very good
- 15 March 2021, Jan Lyczywek: The valley floor is not entirely flat, so not all meadows are equally suitable for landing. The meadow shown here slopes slightly from north to south. Farmers are annoyed by the numerous paraglider landings. Please contact the landowner promptly after landing! Wind-drained areas usually form here due to northerly winds blowing over the northern side of the valley (Fellhorn, Steinplatte, Sonnwendwand). It is therefore advisable to move to the southern side of the valley at an early stage.

When approaching from the west, watch out for unmarked overhead power lines.)
- 23 March 2024, Patrick Misun: Mandatory landing direction 27. Give preference to the southern landing field. There is a large boundary stone with wooden posts between the southern and northern fields. Northern landing field: There are wooden posts in the northern part (upper third) of the landing field. Therefore, use the southern side of the northern landing field.
- 4 March 2021, Charly Giesen: Overhead power line to the west, power line running right along the western edge of the meadow, three meadows

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 09/27
Tour: 2024
Amended: 22.12.2024
Feedback:
- 21 December 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 21 December 2024. One of numerous excellent landing sites in the Westallgäu’s Pre-Alpine landscape, which is generally very suitable for landing. The fact that this particular meadow has made it into the catalogue is likely to have historical reasons: from 1959 to 1982, this was the gliding site of the Westallgäu Air Sports Group from Lindenberg (source: ), and remained a popular meeting place for model aircraft enthusiasts for many years afterwards. In the 1990s and 2000s, gravel pits were dug into the large meadow. But even these have since been filled in, levelled and restored to meadows, so that by 2024 the meadow will certainly be better (!) than it was during the last inspection in 2003. In fact, an airfield could be set up here again. Tour video:

Site visit video:

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 09/27
Tilt: Slightly ascending
Tour: 2024
Amended: 06.06.2024
Feedback:
- 6 June 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 1 June 2024. New meadow “Lengau West”, approx. 500 m west of the existing “Lenagu” meadow listed in the catalogue. Situated right at the head of the Glemmtal valley; the westernmost flat area in the Glemmtal. A narrow, long meadow between houses to the north and large car parks to the south. Rises gently from east to west. However, due to its length, landing is possible in both directions. When approaching from the east (valley-inwards), a curved final approach is necessary, as four houses have been built into the meadow on the right (north), significantly reducing its width. When approaching from the west (out of the valley), you land on gently sloping ground. 100 metres east of the western end of the meadow, a (natural) stream crosses the meadow from north to south. Like a ditch, this cannot be crossed by vehicle. Nevertheless, there remains a distance of 480 metres between the stream to the west and the crossroad to the east. However, due to the various difficulties, it is clearly only a Category B site, though a perfectly acceptable one. In my opinion, it is actually better than the previous ‘Lengau’ meadow. Site inspection video:

Approach from the west is clear, but there is a wooden fence; a gentle incline to the east (5%), a track crosses halfway along (near the houses; can be driven over, shown in red); the northern section next to the road is flat; towards the south-east, the meadow rises steeply (may be helpful when braking); photo taken from the east towards Admonter Reichenstein, with the track shown in red.

- 30 June 2026, Markus Full: This area is probably no longer used as a timber yard, and only half of it is still used as a car park (the area marked in red in the picture). However, there are still a few scattered posts, as thick as tree trunks, remaining from the former timber yard. At the western end of the area marked in yellow in the image, these posts have been cut back to a height of approximately 1–1.5 metres, so that the approach is now reasonably clear again. There is also a low building at the south-western corner. At the eastern end of the area marked in yellow, there is a single, similarly shortened tree-trunk post and a wire fence. In my view, this should again be categorised as C.

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 11/-
Tilt: Slightly ascending
Tour: 2026
Amended: 22.06.2026
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground, power cables and other cables
Feedback:
- 21 June 2026, John Pausder: Inspection on 20 June 2026 by Jan K.: New posts in the meadow
- 8 November 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 8 November 2024. Northernmost meadows: These are the neighbouring meadows, listed in the catalogue to date, situated to the north and south of a tarmac farm track. Approach and landing are only possible from west to east. Slightly ascending in the direction of approach from west to east. Both strips are short and divided into small sections. Individual marker posts along the field boundaries. The strip north of the track is likely unsuitable for landing due to these posts and a barn in the approach path. Although the strip south of the track has a clear approach, it is also only Category C due to various posts – for emergencies only. Inspection video of the northern meadows: Middle meadow: New meadow. Approach and landing only possible from west to east. Approach between two barns. Treat the path between the barns as a threshold. Slight and steady incline in the landing direction from west to east. No cross-slope. Flat surface. Very short, therefore only Category B. Inspection video of the middle meadow: Southern meadow: Meadow opened by Jan Zummach on 29 May 2023 (see also his description below). Approach and landing only possible from west to east. Treat the transverse ditch as a threshold. The surface rises in the direction of landing from west to east. No cross-slope. Distinct bump in the ground. Very short, hence only Category B. The best of the three meadows in Ehrwald. Inspection video of the southern meadow:
- 27.06.2023, Jan Zummach: Another outfield landing area (yellow) near Ehrwald; currently well mown and better than the other published fields as it lacks boundary posts. There is a ditch at the start of the field, followed by a bump after the first 50 metres, so ideally you should touch down shortly after the ditch. The approach is clear and unproblematic. Strong crosswind blowing in from the valley. VGL.
- 19 November 2008, Christian (DD): initial feedback

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 11/29
Tour: 2021
Amended: 06.03.2021
Reported hazards: Electricity and other cables
Feedback:
- 5 March 2021, Josef Enzenebner: The Molln landing field, Holzbau Aigner, is easy to land at from direction 27. You can’t miss the timber yard :-) The field is long enough to ensure you don’t have to fly too close to the settlement. It is essentially completely free of obstacles, with just a small bush well to the south of the landing directions. Further south, however, there may be grazing land, which could potentially extend northwards. The landing field is bordered to the west by a road; if you were to overshoot it, there’s a drop of about 9 metres down an embankment (see elevation profile)! When approaching from direction 09, watch out for the embankment – in other words, fly over the road at a safe altitude! A report on my landing in a Duo-Discus is attached. This text provides useful information and is also an entertaining read :-)

To the south-east, right at the edge of the picture, a ‘Stipfl’ (irrigation connection) can be seen here.

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 12/-
Tilt: Slightly ascending
Tour: 2024
Amended: 25.10.2024
Reported hazards: uneven ground, electrical and other cables
Feedback:
- 25 October 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 24 October 2024. The northernmost of the three fields shown on the map. Sloping upwards from west to east. Approach and landing only possible from west to east. The field is bordered on the north side by a row of tall trees. The field is bordered on the south side by a tall group of trees and the floodlight masts of a sports ground. Length 370 metres, width 65 metres. A power line crosses diagonally through the easternmost quarter; however, as the approach is only possible from west to east anyway, this does not cause any interference. The northern field is an acceptable ‘B’ and is therefore the best of the three fields marked on the map in Ellmau. Site inspection video: The westernmost of the three marked fields: Sloping from west to east. Approach and landing only possible from west to east. Approach over a farmstead lying across the field. Total length 480 metres. However, 280 metres after the farmstead to be flown over, the field is divided by a field boundary marked by individual posts, possibly also by a fence. The terrain also becomes noticeably undulating at this point. The western field is a poor B or a very good C and is therefore the second-best of the three fields marked on the map in Ellmau. Inspection video: The southernmost of the three marked fields: A steep cross-slope from a raised embankment along the main road to the south of the field down to the lower-lying golf course to the north of the field. Extremely undulating and hilly. Approach possible from both directions, but obstructed by trees from both directions. Although the total length is 460 metres, it is divided roughly in the middle by a field boundary. It seemed highly doubtful to us that a landing could be achieved here without causing damage. Even for emergency landings, there are more suitable areas in the immediate vicinity, such as the two other fields marked on the map, or even the fairways of the golf course. The southern field should therefore be classified as a poor Category C or even as unlandable. Inspection video: Overall, Ellmau should nevertheless remain in Category B, as the northernmost field in particular (see above) represents an acceptable Category B.
- 3 March 2021, Christian Gerritzen: Attached is a photo from 2016
- 2 March 2005, Ulrich Plarre: The diggers at the golf course have been cleared away, leaving a narrow strip of the good old meadow for the glider pilots near and alongside the main road!The western alternative runway is still in place, but should be avoided due to the sloping high-voltage pylons on the approach!
- 29 August 2004, Ulrich Plarre: Huge earthworks are taking place!!!

- 30 December 2025, Ute Hoffmann: New: Massive concrete blocks across the entire width divide the meadow into two parts. In addition, approximately 20 m south of the line of concrete blocks, there is a raised steel manhole cover in the centre, which may not be visible when the grass is tall (see photo: view north from the manhole cover). It is still possible to land in both directions: touch down after the concrete blocks, then roll out for approx. 210 m. When landing from the south, do not touch down in the centre, but land in the western or eastern section due to the manhole cover. The eastern section was in better condition. The problem with the meadow is that it is used as a playground and sports field; even during a site visit on 30 December 2025, there were people on the meadow.
- 26 December 2023, Heinz Koch: A huge meadow, with a model aeroplane airfield right in the middle. A circus puts on a show there once a year, but even then there is still enough space to land.

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 16/-
Tilt: Strongly ascending
Tour: 2024
Amended: 03.11.2024
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground, power cables and other cables
Feedback:
- 2 November 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 2 November 2024. 1. Western meadow: Largely unchanged as described. The meadow slopes steeply from the north (road) to the south (Buchensteinwand). Landing is therefore only possible from north to south. The crosswind approach must be taken very low and very close to the opposite slope. Extremely short final approach; touch down after the cross-country path; treat the cross-country path as a threshold. Although it may be possible to overshoot the cross-country path as described, this is certainly not recommended and, given the short final approach, is not at all necessary. At the western end of the path stands a new stable building (built in 2022), but this does not cause any hindrance. The surface quality of the meadow is very good and level. The southern (upper) end of the meadow then becomes increasingly undulating; however, thanks to the good gradient, the roll-out distance remains very short and the southern (upper) end of the meadow is not needed anyway. Inspection video: western meadow. 2. Eastern meadow. Unchanged as described. The meadow slopes steeply from north (road) to south (Buchensteinwand). Landing is therefore only possible from north to south. In the middle of the very wide meadow stands a single barn; this is not a problem. Landing is possible both to the left (east) and to the right (west) of the barn, with ample width available. The crosswind approach must be taken very low and very close to the opposite slope. Very short final approach, slightly longer than for the western meadow. It is NOT possible to overshoot the cross-country path as described, but this is not necessary anyway due to the short final approach. The surface of the meadow is very good and level. The southern (upper) end of the meadow forks, as previously described, into two overshoot strips, separated by a small rectangular copse. Both overshoot strips are undulating; the western one is also very steep (like a ski slope). However, thanks to the good gradient, the roll-out distance remains very short and there is no need to use the southern (upper) end of the field anyway. Inspection video of the eastern field:
- 14 March 2009, Ulrich Plarre: The meadows rise slightly from north to south! A normal approach to the landing area is not possible! The crosswind approach must be very close to the northern slope. The final approach will be very short! At the western landing fields, the crosswind path can be driven over. At the meadows by the Sessel lift, the crosswind path is a bank and cannot be driven over!

- 12 May 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 12 May 2023. Meadows unchanged. Slightly sloping from south to north. Landing possible in both directions. Valley updraft (on days with uniform thermals and no dominant wider-area wind) from the north. The gradient from south to north is so slight that, with a valley wind, landing to the north against the wind is recommended. The strip between the previously marked northern and southern meadows is also suitable for landing. However, this central strip is somewhat bumpier. Power lines pose no problems whatsoever. In terms of quality, given the relatively modest length of each of the three individual meadows, the site rates as a rather poor ‘A’ or a very good ‘B’. Site inspection video:

Amended: 05.09.2021
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground
Feedback:
- 5 September 2021, Daniel Siegele: Landing field situated directly next to the road. A final approach is possible up to 200 metres crosswind of the touchdown point to the west of the meadow, as the valley is very narrow at this point. The recommended landing heading is 170°, as there are an additional 150 metres of meadow before the touchdown point. Please note that for landing 17, marker posts are in place as shown in the pictures. In strong valley winds, a heading of 350° is also possible. Take care when landing 35: there are trees (20 metres high) on the final approach.

Comments:
- 24 April 2022, Ludwig Haslbeck: Blocked off and no longer accessible!
- 24 April 2022, Erik Kurtz: I inspected the meadow on 22 April 2022. A pile of gravel has been dumped in the north, and there is a high barrier standing to one side in the middle. The surface is very uneven. The field is certainly still usable in a pinch (somewhere between Category B and C), but as Belluno airfield is ‘just round the corner’ (12 km away and 60 m lower), this field should be avoided.
- 1 September 2020, CVNE: Meadow OK

Surface: Grass Land
Direction: 17/-
Tilt: Ascending
Tour: 2024
Amended: 25.10.2024
Reported hazards: Electricity and other cables
Feedback:
- 25 October 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 24 October 2024. Both meadows slope steeply from north to south. Landing is therefore only possible from north to south. Although the approach route runs partly over houses and across the busy main road, this is not a problem at all due to the steep slope of the meadow. The western meadow is now divided into two parallel halves by a new farm track running from north to south. The western half of the western meadow (west of the new farm track) is no longer suitable for landing due to earthworks. The eastern half of the western meadow (east of the new track) remains in very good condition and, despite its width being halved by the new track, is still more than wide enough. Category B due to the steep incline, but a very good B! Site inspection video:
- 5 July 2021, David Techmer: The day before yesterday (3 July 2021), I made an off-field landing with an LS6 right next to the meadow in Bocking. The marked-out meadow itself looked just as suitable for landing from the air as it did from the ground. However, I opted for the meadow next to it, as there were no houses or low-lying trees in the final approach. That said, the meadow I chose is a bit shorter. (Unlike in the aerial photograph, it had also been mown all the way across this year). It cannot be compared to Category A outlanding meadows due to the slope of the terrain. But it is definitely wide and long enough to bring the glider down safely. The power lines shown on the maps are all correct. However, due to the sloping terrain towards the centre of the valley, the northern pylons and the power line are not the main obstacle affecting the approach altitude. The row of trees and the houses are also situated on lower ground than the meadows and are therefore less of an obstruction than one might expect. The country road is very busy. The farmer is also very friendly. Both the glider and the pilot emerged completely unscathed.
- 19 November 2008, Christian (DD): initial feedback

- 14 May 2023, Johannes Schwemmer: Visit by car on 12 May 2023 during the Unterwössen cross-country flying week. Meadow Plan A (blue) is easy to approach from the south-east. From our perspective, it’s a straightforward A-meadow. Video: (Even though Dorfgastein is mentioned here, it is actually Hofgastein.) Sat-nav destination: ‘Parkplatz Bertahof Bad Hofgastein’. Just south of the car park, which is on the west side of the main road, there is an entrance to the meadow on the east side of the road, which even seemed suitable for trailers. Alternative meadow (yellow): shorter but usable.
- 15 March 2021, Roland Henz: Alternative to the north: A lovely, long meadow. The owner isn’t particularly keen on landings, though, as paragliders and hang-gliders are regularly parked there too! When landing, it is therefore advisable to get in touch with the owner via the Stubnerbauer farmyard; the Stubnerhof is right across the road from the meadow and also offers farm holidays. There are other ‘designated’ meadows in the valley.

To the west, there is a major high-voltage power line running parallel to the landing field. To the east, the field is bordered by a solid pasture fence that does not cause any interference!

Surface: Grass runway
Direction: -/33
Gradient: Slightly ascending
Inspection: 2025
Amended: 31 March 2025
Reported hazards: uneven ground
Feedback:
- 22 March 2025, Nora Geusen: as described below
- 25 March 2023, Jan Lyczywek: inspection on 24 March 2023. UL runway with a windsock. Rises from south-east to north-west; landing is therefore only possible from south-east to north-west. Two white, cone-shaped landing markers indicate the threshold. Do not touch down before the threshold, as the ground in front of it is uneven. However, do not touch down too far beyond the threshold either, as the ground becomes increasingly uneven during a long roll-out uphill towards the north-west. The best spot is the first 150 metres beyond the threshold. From the air, the field looks patchy in colour, uneven and therefore worse than it actually is. However, there are also many agricultural fields all around. It’s a difficult choice; with the UL runway, at least you know what you’re getting. In March 2023, the runway was rather poorly maintained, with a few small thorny bushes here and there. Site inspection video:

At the end of runway 24, the grass is currently short (should allow for a smooth touchdown without damage from the gravel)
- 13 March 2025, Mathias Schunk: Inspection on 12 March 2025. Surface: gravel runway. Conditions unchanged with a pile of stones to the east

- 1 April 2022, Jürgen Wisbacher: Inspection on 30 March 2022. The scree pile at the eastern end of the site is approx. 10 m high. Consequently, when approaching from the west, the landing area becomes very short. Image 1 shows the position of the top of the scree pile. Image 2 shows the scree pile photographed from the east. Approach from the west only in strong breezes; otherwise, land from the lake heading east.

- 15 March 2021, Thomas Münchow: The landing area exists for historical reasons. It is the former Flintsbach gliding airfield, but the old runway has since been divided by paths, meaning that the original landing approach is no longer possible. The meadows here are suitable for landing, but are practically within the traffic pattern of the ‘new’ Brannenburg airfield. Provided you still have enough altitude for a reconnaissance circuit, you can also land at Brannenburg. IGC from Condor. I think we should not include this field in the catalogue.
- 23 February 2021, Tomasz Królik: I was in Flintsbach three weeks ago. The hangar is still standing, but there is no longer a runway there. The fields are suitable for landing. The Rosenheim Aviation Club can confirm this information.

Surface: Grass runway
Direction: 15/33
Inspected: 2000
Updated: 19 March 2023
Feedback:
- 16 February 2021, Robert Zinnecker: A good landing option when all else fails. Only French or English may be spoken on the airfield frequency 123.50. The licence now explicitly permits us to carry out up to 15 take-offs per day, with the possibility of a short-term increase to up to 20 take-offs for meteorological reasons, subject to subsequent compensation. Previously, only a maximum of 15 ‘flight movements’ were permitted. We will therefore, for the foreseeable future, never accept more than 13–14 aircraft at any one time at our airfield. This will hopefully ensure that the friendly, informal and peaceful flying operations at Sainte-Croix are maintained for a long time to come. For formal reasons, the permit is held by the French club, ‘Aero-Club du Lac de Sainte Croix du Verdon’, which for this reason works in close cooperation with our Aero-Club Provence e.V. One inconvenient condition of our licence is the requirement to register all pilots and aircraft using this private airfield. We must submit this registration annually at the start of the season, in the spring. Anyone who is not registered is not permitted to fly at the airfield! Contact: Aero-Club Provence e.V. — Karl Heidler, Heideweg 108, 49477 Ibbenbüren. ---------------------------------. Tel: 0 54 51 - 562 88 88. Email: karl@heidler-ibb.de.

Amended: 29 June 2026
Feedback:
- 14 February 2026, Jan Lyczywek: Landing and take-off on 18 June 2025 with a Duo Discus. The airfield is perfectly flat, long, with an unobstructed approach, and has a parallel valley wind. Definitely Category A. On thermal days without a dominant long-range wind, there is a strong valley wind from north-north-west. The runway was perfectly mown very short, but there was tall grass to the left and right of the runway. Although there is no tow plane on site, F-tow is possible if a tow plane can be organised from Grenoble Le Versoud, Challes-les-Eaux or, as in our case, from Barcelonette. Failed take-off with landing: Restart with a long tow: [ WeGlide flight ]
- 26 August 2025, Jonas Blahnik: Airfield, good landing options

- 17 February 2021, Wolfgang Frenzel: The airfield is generally open from April to October. Landing direction 330, not 320. Grass runway: 350m long, 30m wide. Landings must be made exclusively in direction 330. The airfield rises by approx. 30m towards the north. Take-offs must be made exclusively in direction 150 (tow-in / self-launch) or in direction 240 (when using a winch). Do not touch down before the landing threshold (asphalt cross-runway).

- 17 July 2021, Marcus Neubronner: The Wehr 2 landing field is situated on the Dinkelberg, to the west and above Wehr.
- 17 July 2021, Marcus Neubronner: All details are included in the PDF. An absolutely trouble-free landing area. It is preferable to land on the 36, due to the slightly sloping terrain. Please note: This is a paramotor flying site! It is therefore mandatory to always announce your landing by radio in advance (even if no one is visible on the field), on 122.555. After landing, please contact the owner, Christoph Nägele, on 0049 151 1078 1915!

en\u001fOffenbar ist am Vortag (15. Apri: Apparently, a pilot landed on the Farchant-Süd meadow the day before (15 April 2011). Due to the valley wind system, a landing heading north, towards the houses, is preferable. If possible, touch down on the ridge that runs across the meadow.
en\u001fOnly land on the north side of t: Only land on the north side of the area!
en\u001fOnly one landing direction uphil: Only one landing direction uphill to the northwest. Final approach parallel to the dam wall. Edges of the field bordered by ditches. Meadow. 240m long diagonally.
en\u001fParallel high voltage power line: Parallel high voltage power line 50m east of the runway.
en\u001fPath number two clear to overrun: Path number two clear to overrun.
en\u001fPower line on the road crosses a: Power line on the road crosses at the southern end
en\u001fProblemlose Außenlandung auf der: Smooth off-field landing on the meadow described on 24 June 2023. Approach over the roof of the farmhouse from north to south. Long enough and very level. Came to a halt before the incline. Slight incline over the last 200 metres or so, so landing from the north is recommended.
en\u001fRadio tower north in the approac: Radio tower north in the approach of the middle field!
en\u001fRechts und links des Feldes Pfos: There are posts to the right and left of the field. It cannot be ruled out that some of the posts may have been moved. Take care when approaching the huts and watch out for the railway cables.
en\u001fRiding facility. Possibly of obs: Riding facility. Possibly of obstacle set up!
en\u001fSW/NE Attention ligne HT 1km au: SW/NE: Caution – high-voltage power line 1 km to the south-west. Variable terrain: stones and grassland.
en\u001fSW/NE Prairie. Reco a pied indis: SW/NE Prairie. A recce on foot is essential.
en\u001fSchöne Landewiese im engen Großa: A lovely meadow in the narrow Großarltal valley
en\u001fSehr gute Wiese mit niedrigem Be: A very good field with low vegetation. The field is used as a landing site by a paragliding club, although, according to local sources, this is no longer a frequent occurrence. It is completely flat in the direction of landing, and when approaching from the south, the only obstacle is a low-hanging power line. The field is fenced off, but there is a gate providing easy access. I landed there with an LS4 – landing heading south – and came to a stop whilst still level with the houses. [ WeGlide flight ]
en\u001fSehr steile Wiese, aber ok: A very steep meadow, but it’s fine
en\u001fSenke in der Mitte --> meistens: A dip in the middle --> usually wet. Friendly people, always a cold beer to hand
en\u001fSeveral fields separated by catt: Several fields separated by cattle fences
en\u001fSieht gut aus! Gras momentan kni: Looks good! The grass is knee-high at the moment.
en\u001fSind mit dem Motorsegler drüberg: We flew over it in the motor glider. It looked good.
en\u001fSpannungsleitungen sind in der N: There are power lines nearby. If you’re approaching from the north, you’ll need to fly round a hill south of the runway. With bulkier aircraft > 18m, this could prove a bit of a challenge. The meadow itself, however, is fine.
en\u001fSteel bars and bumps in the east: Steel bars and bumps in the eastern area!
en\u001fStrong valley wind from the nort: Strong valley wind from the north
en\u001fSuper Wiese, fast schon Flugplat: A brilliant field, almost like an airfield. The grass wasn’t too long, but the ground was very soft in places. Even so, the field was accessible even with a trailer. It’s important to keep an eye on the small model aeroplane airfield next door, so that no model aeroplanes end up on the approach path.
en\u001fTake the meadow in Tösens when p: Take the meadow in Tösens when possible, Water ditches!
en\u001fTalboden nicht durchgängig eben,: The valley floor is not entirely flat, so not all meadows are equally suitable for landing. The meadow shown here slopes slightly from north to south. Farmers are annoyed by the numerous paraglider landings. Please contact the landowner promptly after landing! Wind-drained areas usually form here due to northerly winds blowing over the northern side of the valley (Fellhorn, Steinplatte, Sonnwendwand). It is therefore advisable to move to the southern side of the valley at an early stage.
en\u001fTaleinwind (an Thermiktagen ohne: Valley wind (on days with thermals but no wider regional wind) from the north.
en\u001fTeilweise Maisanbau. Das hier ge: Partly used for maize cultivation. The field shown here lies on the western edge of the Innsbruck Control Zone (CTR), just just outside it! Valley wind (on thermal days with no wider regional wind) from the east.
en\u001fTeilweise Maisanbau? Gesamtlänge: Partially used for maize cultivation? Total length 500 m, divided by two farm tracks/wheel tracks (possibly passable?). In the eastern third, a power line crosses diagonally; this can be flown under when landing from the west. Valley wind (on thermal days with no wider-area wind) from the east. When a strong southerly foehn is blowing, the wind is from the west!
en\u001fThe field on the south side are: The field on the south side are rising in east direction!
en\u001fThere are a lot of fences and ca: There are a lot of fences and cables on the other meadow!
en\u001fTouch down in the second half af: Touch down in the second half after the path!
en\u001fTwo landable directions. Each ab: Two landable directions. Each about 400m landing distance. Landing is always uphill. The edges of the field are marked with clearly visible obstacles (path, forest). Meadow. Uncritical. The southern runway may have to be followed slightly with a left turn while taxiing.
en\u001fTwo landable directions. Highest: Two landable directions. Highest point of the field in the middle right next to the radio mast. Terrain steeply sloping to the southwest. Outlanding field is at 920m MSL. Meadow. High forest in approach from northwest. Towards southeast at the end of the field strong descent. 400m total length.
en\u001fUnproblematische Landewiese im G: A suitable landing site in the Gail Valley.
en\u001fUnproblematisches Feld falls man: This is not a problem if you are not travelling to either Brannenburg or Kufstein.
en\u001fUnverändert sehr gut. Zahlreiche: Still very good. Plenty of good options all round; it’s actually a flat, open landscape. The message of this catalogue entry is probably more that model aerodromes are usually too short, too narrow and littered with obstacles such as safety fences, so the surrounding fields are generally a better option.
en\u001fUpdate zur letzten Meldung mit F: Update on the last report with a photo (metal structure in the field). A newspaper article states that a solar power plant is planned here
en\u001fUrsprüngliche Wiese mit Pfosten: The original meadow has been divided into two by posts, making it difficult to land without causing damage. In addition, a new hut has been built by the roadside. One meadow further west, there is a maize field with very good landing conditions; see picture
en\u001fViele, viele Zaunstempel in der: Lots and lots of fence posts in the meadow. The nearby village of Sillian is better
en\u001fVogelscheuchen in Rauen Mengen a: Scares in droves in the southern meadow belonging to the two of them.
en\u001fVorbeigefahren (aber nicht angeh: Drove past (but did not stop) on 28 April 2023. The northern field (north of the B 178) is still ploughed up. The southern meadow (south of the B 178 main road) is somewhat shorter, but still grade A. It slopes upwards from west to east, so the landing is from west to east. Numerous even better meadows can be found in the Tiroler Ache valley, both to the south towards Kirchdorf and to the north in the Hagertal.
en\u001fWatch for hangar at the beginnin: Watch for hangar at the beginning of runway 09.
en\u001fWater ditches s are placed acros: Water ditches s are placed across landing direction!!!
en\u001fWeiterhin alles gut. Viele Felde: Everything’s still fine. Plenty of fields, with a choice depending on the cut and vegetation. Please note: there are a few posts here and there.
en\u001fWeiterhin sehr gut landbar, 06.0: Still very easy to land on; viewed on 6 June 2023
en\u001fWeiterhin sehr gut, das Gras kön: Still very good; the grass might be a bit long as long as it hasn’t been mown
en\u001fWenn man Richtung Norden anflieg: When approaching from the north, there is a bump in the ground in the first quarter of the field, which you should watch out for.
en\u001fWenn`s mal nicht nach Zeltweg re: If you can’t quite make it to Zeltweg.
en\u001fWestliche Alternative:. Taleinwi: Western alternative: Valley wind (on days with thermals but no wind from further afield) from the south-east.
en\u001fWestliche Hälfte der Wiese ist a: The western half of the meadow is currently a maize field
en\u001fWestlicher Teil momentan Getreid: The western section is currently planted with crops. The eastern section is a meadow. The eastern section looks better. As well as the main slope, there are also varying lateral slopes. There is a pole in the upper section between the western and eastern sections. (See photo). It’s certainly landable, but not without its challenges. Access route: A steep (the steepness doesn’t show up in the photo) forest track, partly washed out. => It’ll be tricky with a VW Polo.;-)
en\u001fWhen you roll over path there wi: When you roll over path there will be a hard impact!
en\u001fWie die anderen schon geschriebe: As others have already mentioned, power lines are clearly visible thanks to the red balls. You should land as far to the left of the hall as possible, as the hill mentioned there is a bit of a nuisance if you land right on it.
en\u001fWiese am 18.2.2024 in einem gute: The meadow was in good condition on 18 February 2024
en\u001fWiese ist gut landbar: The meadow is easy to land on
en\u001fWiese kurz und etwas wellig, abe: The meadow is short and slightly undulating, but suitable for landing
en\u001fWiese mit stellenweise starkem G: A meadow with steep slopes in places. However, it’s easy to land on the crest. Best to head south.
en\u001fWiese o.k. ( kein Hindernis in d: Meadow OK (no obstacles in the meadow)
en\u001fWiese ok. - 250m östlich ist bei: Meadow OK. – About 250 metres to the east, there is another landing meadow with a similar approach direction (15). Currently (9 April 2022), there are no fences on the meadows.
en\u001fWiese unverändert sehr gut. Land: The meadow remains in very good condition. Arrived on 23 September 2022. Link:
en\u001fWiese wie beschrieben in Ordnung: The meadow is fine, as described.
en\u001fWiese wie beschrieben. Bodenwell: Meadow as described. Bumps in the central section are not a problem. Possibly used for grazing. Approach 29 offers more flexibility in terms of approach routes, but both directions are perfectly feasible. The access road is steep and has deep potholes!
en\u001fWiese wie beschrieben. Im Süden: Meadow as described. Steep to the south, with tall trees in the distance. Sloping. Flattest in the centre
en\u001fWiesen sind in beiden Richtungen: Meadows can be landed on from either direction
Meadows remain in very good condition.
en\u001fWiesen werden teilweise durch mo: Some of the meadows are blocked by mobile chicken coops.
en\u001fWiesen wie beschrieben. Leitunge: Meadows as described. Power lines at the edge of the meadow are not a problem. Approach possible from both directions. For both landing directions, aim for the second meadow (as seen from the landing direction) as the landing area.
Meadows as described; uneven. Give preference to the western meadow: slopes gently in the direction of the landing area, but there are tall trees on the approach. Eastern meadow: very uneven, with bumps and a sloping gradient; there is a path in front of the marked area – do not drive over it!
en\u001fWind from the valley is tailwind: Wind from the valley is tailwind!
Wind from the valley is tailwind! Bumpy!
en\u001fWith valley wind (east) also cro: With valley wind (east) also crosswise landable!
en\u001fWollte Dir noch schreiben dass d: I just wanted to let you know that the off-piste area in Galtür is fine (I’m in Paznaun at the moment).
en\u001fYou can roll over all paths!: You can roll over all paths!
en\u001fYou can roll over the crossing p: You can roll over the crossing path!
en\u001fZA Attention aux lignes electriq: ZA: Watch out for the power lines running through the area.
en\u001fZA Attention petite ligne electr: ZA Caution: low-voltage power line. Tall grass. Pasture. Cereals.
en\u001fZA Attention petite lignes elect: ZA: Caution: low-voltage power lines. Tall grass. Pasture. Cereals.
en\u001fZA Attentions selon axe: colline: ZA Points to note depending on the route: hills to drive over or along. Possible issues with the landowner.
en\u001fZA Bon etat a W du champ contrai: ZA Good condition to the west of the field, despite appearances. Slight slope. Unobstructed approach.
en\u001fZA Brise dans axe different des: ZA Brise is on a different axis to the fields. Watch out for low-hanging power lines.
en\u001fZA Champ Cereales. Attention lig: ZA Champ Cereals. Beware of power lines.
ZA Choose according to the crops.
en\u001fZA Choisir en fct cultures. Atte: ZA: Choose according to crop type. Take care to avoid high-voltage power lines and wind turbines (h=100m). A site inspection on foot is recommended.
en\u001fZA Choisir en fct cultures. Autr: Agricultural zone: Choose according to crops. Another agricultural zone 3 km to the south-east.
en\u001fZA Choisir en fct cultures. Pb p: ZA: Choose according to crops. Possible issues with the landowner.
en\u001fZA Facile / Attention parapentes: ZA Easy / Caution: paragliders / ZA extends southwards towards Faverge.
en\u001fZA Point haut E= Route E/W qui p: ZA High point E = E/W road passing through St Jean. Fields slope gently downwards to the north and south. The clearly visible high-voltage power line is not an obstacle.
en\u001fZA Reco a pied indispensable. Cu: Essential on-foot reconnaissance. Crops may be present.
en\u001fZA Reco a pied indispensable. Li: A site survey on foot is essential. A high-voltage power line is currently under construction.
en\u001fZA Terrain ULM inutilisable. Zon: ZA: Microlight airfield unusable. Landing area to the south of the river.
ZA Vaste ZA. Watch out for maize. Variable wind. High-voltage power line.
en\u001fZA Zone cultivee attention aux c: ZA Farmland – watch out for the lavender fields.
en\u001fZum Bauernhof nach Nordosten hin: Slightly uphill towards the farm to the north-east.
en\u001fZustand August 2024 unverändert: Situation as at August 2024: unchanged from 2023
en\u001fZusätzlich neuerdings Holzpfoste: In addition, wooden posts have recently been installed.
en\u001fZwei Möglichkeiten:. A) Landung: Two options: A) Land across the valley on runway 14, right-hand approach. Do not touch down too early; there are wooden posts halfway across the meadow. B) Alternatively, runway 09 is also possible; better final approach, a slightly longer field at an angle, and a slight incline.
en\u001fabout 30cm high thin posts in th: about 30cm high thin posts in the middle of the field
en\u001fabsolute Notwiese stark ansteige: An absolute nightmare of a runway – very steep (I’ve landed there before) – not to be recommended!!! I just didn’t want to keep this from you,
en\u001fauf der wiese befindet sich nun: There is now a vineyard on the meadow
en\u001fca 30cm hohe, 2cm breite Pfosten: Posts approximately 30 cm high and 2 cm wide, positioned in the centre of the field’s width. The north-east side is bordered by a 45-degree slope (see picture). A landing was made there on 3 October 2021 without causing any damage.
en\u001fca. 360m Lang ab dem Heustadl, A: Approx. 360 m long from the hay barn; approach from the south-east over the village, possibly also from the opposite direction;
en\u001fcurrent line in approach area. S: current line in approach area. Single landing strips, in between stakes and fences!
en\u001fditch in the middle of the meado: ditch in the middle of the meadow
en\u001fdue local wind systems landing d: due local wind systems landing direction 33!
en\u001fforested hill in approach, fly a: forested hill in approach, fly around!
en\u001fmomentan super. Junge Maispflänz: Great at the moment. Young maize plants. Not recommended later in the year when the maize is tall.
en\u001fnormal approach not possible! Sh: normal approach not possible! Short end approach!
en\u001fnorth alternative is better to l: north alternative is better to land
en\u001fnur kurzer Endanflug möglich, ab: Only a short final approach is possible, but there are no longer any obstacles in the meadow.
en\u001fnur vom Boden aus besichtigt. An: Viewed only from the ground. Approach is made towards rising terrain. There may be pegs in the field or at the edge
en\u001fprivater UL Platz mit befestigte: Private microlight airfield with a paved runway in the heart of the moor
en\u001frolling over asphaltic crossing: rolling over asphaltic crossing road on eastern part possible
en\u001fseveral fields lined up, lee eff: several fields lined up, lee effects with north wind!
en\u001fstarkes Gefälle vom Wald zur Ort: A steep gradient from the forest down to the village. Approach over the village in normal wind conditions.
en\u001funebener Boden, Bodenhindernisse: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground
uneven ground, obstacles on the ground, power cables and other cables
en\u001funebener Boden, Strom-/andere Le: uneven ground, electrical and other cables
en\u001fvon Ost nach West leicht ansteig: Rising slightly from east to west; the preferred landing direction is therefore from east to west.
en\u001fvon Osten leichter anfliegbar: easier to approach from the east
en\u001fwhen you approach from the south: when you approach from the south to the north watch out for a big crossing bump! Farchant Nord is much better.
en\u001fyou can roll over the paths!: you can roll over the paths!
en\u001fÖstlich des Dorfes Silz, zwische: East of the village of Silz, between the motorway and the railway line. A long, flat area, alternating between meadow and field, so be flexible. Both landing directions are possible. Quality: easy to approach, but the usable area may be short.
en\u001fÜber LateNightSoaring wurde ich: It was through LateNightSoaring that I first learnt about the outlying landing strips. Christoph and I from AkaFliegFreiburg flew past them yesterday in our motor glider – this would be my preferred landing spot. It’s quite large and easy to spot. You might even catch a bit of an updraft at one of the quarries – and if not, it’s still a good place to land.

I found out about the off-field landing strips through LateNightSoaring. We flew past them yesterday in the motor glider – it looks like a good meadow – though it’s quite narrow and there’s a winding footpath nearby.
evtl bessere Wiese etwas noerdlicher ent: There may be a better meadow a little further north along the road, undulating, with high power lines to the north. (Editor’s note: The middle field, now marked in blue)
fr\u001f- Sieht noch ok aus. - einzige W: - Ça a l'air encore correct. - Seule prairie de la vallée. - Au sud, une ligne à haute tension ; au nord, une ligne téléphonique. - La route dans la vallée est très, très étroite. - Avec une remorque, je pense qu'on ne peut y accéder que par le nord-ouest ; les routes traversant les villages depuis le sud-est sont plutôt trop étroites.
fr\u001f01.01.2023, Mathias Schunk: In m: 1er janvier 2023, Mathias Schunk : À mon avis, compte tenu de l'étroitesse de la vallée, cette prairie ne devrait pas être prise en compte dans la planification stratégique et ne devrait être envisagée que comme une solution de secours.
fr\u001f01.05.2024, Holger Leicht: Besch: 1er mai 2024, Holger Leicht : La description est toujours d'actualité. Il y a énormément de tranchées et de poteaux.
fr\u001f01.05.2026, Benedikt Bursian: La: 1er mai 2026, Benedikt Bursian : Atterrissage le 22 avril 2026, l'option reste très intéressante. Des pylônes d'éclairage ont été installés sur la trajectoire d'approche venant de l'ouest, mais ils sont faciles à survoler. [ Vol WeGlide ]
fr\u001f01.07.2021, Konrad Mahlknecht: A: 1er juillet 2021, Konrad Mahlknecht : J'ai atterri le 26 juin 2021 avec notre ASK21. Deux points méritent une attention particulière : 1. Il peut y avoir de la circulation sur la route devant le pré ; je recommande donc d'approcher avec une marge suffisante pour pouvoir survoler d'éventuels véhicules. 2. Le pré présente une pente d'environ 1 m, surtout du côté droit après la route. Il faut en tenir compte lors de la manœuvre d’arrondi. À part cela, le pré était parfait. Peu de végétation. Longueur suffisante. Nous nous sommes déjà posés à peu près au milieu du pré.
fr\u001f01.07.2022, Harald Franz-tannich: 1er juillet 2022, Harald Franz-Tannich : Le pré situé parallèlement à celui-ci, entre le terrain d'atterrissage et la B307, est également praticable.

1er juillet 2022, Harald Franz-Tannich : Lors de ma visite du 29 juillet 2022, la prairie située au sud, parallèle à la rangée d'arbres, m'a semblé plus propice à l'atterrissage. Aucune trace de la clôture (mentionnée en 2021).
1er juillet 2022, Harald Franz-Tannich : Le sens d'entretien de la prairie change parfois ; actuellement (photo du 29 juin 2022), il s'oriente plutôt vers la direction 33.

1er juillet 2022, Harald Franz-Tannich : Ne commencer qu'à partir du chemin qui passe en face de l'Almhof. Parallèlement au chemin se trouve (photo du 29 juin 2022) une clôture électrique légère destinée aux pâturages.
1er juillet 2022, Harald Franz-tannich : Toujours accessible comme indiqué.
fr\u001f01.07.2025, Dr. Arno Hutter: Bei: 1er juillet 2025, Dr Arno Hutter : Vu en passant le 27 mai : en raison de travaux routiers/d'assainissement ? Actuellement inaccessible.
fr\u001f01.08.2005, Christian Holzner: D: 1er août 2005, Christian Holzner : Actuellement inaccessible, car entièrement exploité !
1er août 2005, Christian Holzner : La conduite manquante à l'extrémité sud est désormais indiquée sur la photo aérienne !
fr\u001f01.08.2005, Christian Holzner: E: 1er août 2005, Christian Holzner : À supprimer, car toute la prairie a été exploitée l'année dernière et l'est à nouveau cette année. Tschagguns n'est qu'à quelques kilomètres de là.
fr\u001f01.08.2005, Christian Holzner: I: 1er août 2005, Christian Holzner : Les travaux ont entre-temps commencé sur le terrain de golf (voir photo). Il reste toutefois encore suffisamment de prairies tout autour.
fr\u001f01.08.2005, Christoph Lhota: Die: 1er août 2005, Christoph Lhota : Cette aire d'atterrissage d'urgence ne mérite même pas ce nom. Les grosses ondulations et les clôtures à bétail transforment l'atterrissage en véritable cauchemar. Mieux vaut opter pour un amerrissage !
fr\u001f01.11.2022, Stefan Jahnke: Alle: 1er novembre 2022, Stefan Jahnke : Toutes les prairies situées au sud de la route transversale sont inutilisables. Les prairies du nord sont en meilleur état, mais il est préférable de choisir Nassereith Nord.
fr\u001f01.11.2022, Stefan Jahnke: Gute: 1er novembre 2022, Stefan Jahnke : Belle prairie, mais dont la longueur est actuellement réduite de moitié à cause du maïs. Ligne à haute tension qui s'approche depuis l'ouest.
1er novembre 2022, Stefan Jahnke : De belles prairies étendues, mais aussi beaucoup de maïs. Pour l'instant, seule une grande prairie est exploitable.
1er novembre 2022, Stefan Jahnke : Belle et vaste prairie. Ligne à haute tension approchant par le sud.
fr\u001f01.11.2022, Stefan Jahnke: Sehr: 1er novembre 2022, Stefan Jahnke : Piste très étroite à l'approche par l'est. Lignes à haute tension à gauche et rangée d'arbres à droite. Si possible, effectuer une approche courte et raide après les obstacles, puis atterrir un peu plus loin. La prairie s'élargit nettement vers l'ouest.

1er novembre 2022, Stefan Jahnke : Approche très raide depuis l'ouest, possible uniquement avec un petit monoplace. L'approche depuis l'est est bonne. Les prairies propices à l'atterrissage sont séparées au milieu, à hauteur du nouveau potager/de la nouvelle serre. Le terrain y est très bosselé et comporte de nombreux poteaux.
fr\u001f02.02.2025, Philip Heinrich: Heu: 02/02/2025, Philip Heinrich : Je l'ai emprunté deux fois cette année. Une famille très sympathique ! Je ne m'y engage que dans la deuxième moitié, celle marquée d'une flèche vers le nord. C'est facile si on suit le chemin. La partie marquée d'une croix est, à mon avis, trop risquée. Le terrain est accidenté et par endroits caillouteux !
fr\u001f02.02.2025, Philip Heinrich: Sup: 2 février 2025, Philip Heinrich : Super Landbar. Le but était grand ouvert.
2 février 2025, Philip Heinrich : Super, accessible depuis l'ouest. L'accès s'est fait sans problème en passant par la ferme. Il suffit de passer sous le poteau électrique.
fr\u001f02.02.2026, Philip Heinrich: Bin: 02/02/2026, Philip Heinrich : J'y ai atterri en janvier. Une belle prairie, sans turbulences ni autres perturbations notables. L'espace n'est toutefois pas illimité. Avec le Ventus, il me restait encore environ 150 m de marge. Le fermier est sympathique et coopératif.
fr\u001f02.02.2026, Philip Heinrich: Ich: 02/02/2026, Philip Heinrich : J'y ai atterri en 2024 et en 2025. Je la classerais entre A et B. Il est préférable d'atterrir à partir de la deuxième moitié de la prairie, qui est plus plate. À cet endroit, elle est tout à fait praticable, sans ondulations notables. L'approche au-dessus des maisons doit être effectuée à une altitude suffisante.
fr\u001f02.02.2026, Philip Heinrich: Sie: 2 février 2026, Philip Heinrich : Vu du ciel, c'est toujours aussi beau
fr\u001f02.03.2005, Ulrich Plarre: Die B: 2 mars 2005, Ulrich Plarre : Les pelleteuses ont quitté le terrain de golf et ont laissé aux pilotes de planeur, à proximité et parallèlement à la route nationale, une étroite bande de cette bonne vieille prairie !La piste de secours ouest existe toujours, mais il convient de l'éviter en approche en raison des lignes à haute tension en biais !
fr\u001f02.03.2021, Flugsport-Wien: mehr: 2 mars 2021, Flugsport-Wien : plusieurs champs et prairies alignés les uns à côté des autres, effets de sillage par vent du nord !
fr\u001f02.03.2021, Klaus Schwärzler: Al: 2 mars 2021, Klaus Schwärzler : Tout est resté comme avant, comme décrit ! Photos prises depuis le chemin agricole à l'ouest de Landefeld
fr\u001f02.03.2021, Paul Bernhard: leich: 2 mars 2021, Paul Bernhard : légèrement en pente, préférence pour la parcelle 1 ; les parcelles 1 et 2 sont généralement cultivées différemment ; la parcelle 3 est une parcelle de secours
fr\u001f02.04.2022, Gerhard Hiemesch: Me: 2 avril 2022, Gerhard Hiemesch : Mon atterrissage en campagne, le 10 juillet 2021, s'est effectué dans le pré situé au sud-ouest de cet endroit, et j'ai échappé de justesse à un accident. En inspectant le champ balisé, j'ai constaté que cela aurait clairement été le meilleur choix. Le terrain d’atterrissage est légèrement surélevé et est utilisé par les pilotes de parapente. Un petit manchon à vent se trouve à l’angle sud. Comme toujours avec les terrains d’atterrissage, il est bien sûr possible que le terrain ne soit pas exempt d’obstacles.
fr\u001f02.04.2023, Michael Rüdisser: In: 2 avril 2023, Michael Rüdisser : À Schlins, j'ai privilégié la prairie située au sud-ouest de l'aérodrome de modélisme, y compris la piste d'atterrissage de celui-ci. Drapeau et manche à vent sur place !

2 avril 2023, Michael Rüdisser : À Schruns, j'atterrirais dans le sens de la vallée et non perpendiculairement à celle-ci. En cas de foehn, vers l'intérieur de la vallée, et en cas de bise, vers l'extérieur de la vallée. Manche à vent près de l'aire d'atterrissage des parapentes. Attention au terrain accidenté et à quelques poteaux marron de 0,5 m de haut au sud-ouest de la zone d'atterrissage des parapentes.
fr\u001f02.04.2025, Achim Holzmann: Heut: 2 avril 2025, Achim Holzmann : Je me suis rendu sur place aujourd’hui. La prairie est en bon état.
fr\u001f02.12.2022, Herbert Novak: Futte: 2 décembre 2022, Herbert Novak : prairie fourragère (production de balles d'ensilage) ; fauchée 2 à 3 fois par an ; au printemps, avant la première fauche (vers la fin mai), l'herbe peut être assez haute ; ligne électrique aérienne en bordure sud-est de la prairie ; vent au sol soufflant généralement du sud-est ; les deux directions d'approche sont facilement sélectionnables.
fr\u001f03.03.2021, Charly Giesen: Leitu: 3 mars 2021, Charly Giesen : Une ligne électrique traverse la prairie
fr\u001f03.03.2021, Christian Gerritzen:: 3 mars 2021, Christian Gerritzen : Voici quelques photos datant de 2016
3 mars 2021, Christian Gerritzen : Voici une photo datant de 2016
3 mars 2021, Christian Gerritzen : Les vols en avion en bois sont très rares. Il faut plutôt considérer cet endroit comme un simple terrain d'atterrissage en campagne.
3 mars 2021, Christian Gerritzen : L'aérodrome de vol libre est toujours en activité. Mais généralement uniquement le week-end. Pas de possibilité de remorquage.
fr\u001f03.04.2023, Ernst Wolfgang Knoch: 3 avril 2023, Ernst Wolfgang Knoch : aucune modification
fr\u001f03.07.2022, Daniel Konrad: Baum: 3 juillet 2022, Daniel Konrad : l'arbre au milieu du champ n'existe plus. (koepp_03). Vue sur la clairière d'atterrissage depuis les directions 10 (koepp_01) et 28 (koepp_02) – à environ 50-60 m AGL chacune.
fr\u001f03.07.2022, Martin Dietrich: Wie: 3 juillet 2022, Martin Dietrich : La prairie est en bon état ; le chemin situé à l'ouest de la photo est de plain-pied et ne comporte pas de marche.
fr\u001f03.07.2022, Martin Dietrich: am: 3 juillet 2022, Martin Dietrich : le 2 juillet 2021, du maïs a été cultivé
fr\u001f03.07.2026, Andreas Winhard: Aus: 3 juillet 2026, Andreas Winhard : atterrissage en campagne le 5 juillet 2025. Prairie en excellent état. Atterrissage en direction de l'est. La pente vers le centre de la vallée est moins prononcée que ne le laissent supposer les photos. [ Vol WeGlide ]
fr\u001f03.08.2025, Ute Baranowski: Besi: 3 août 2025, Ute Baranowski : visite le 3 août ; la prairie n'a pas changé
fr\u001f03.09.2021, Florian Galler: In d: 3 septembre 2021, Florian Galler : Au milieu de la zone se trouvent deux poteaux en bois d'environ 2 m de haut (probablement des perchoirs pour les rapaces). Si l'on atterrit près de la route, cela ne devrait pas poser de problème.
